types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1FeatureOnlineStore
types.description: Vertex AI Feature Online Store provides a centralized repository for serving ML features and embedding indexes at low latency. The Feature Online Store is a top-level container.

fields.name: optimized
fields.description: Optimized storage type

fields.name: name
fields.description: Identifier. Name of the FeatureOnlineStore. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/featureOnlineStores/{featureOnlineStore}`

fields.name: createTime
fields.description: Output only. Timestamp when this FeatureOnlineStore was created.

fields.name: updateTime
fields.description: Output only. Timestamp when this FeatureOnlineStore was last updated.

fields.name: etag
fields.description: Optional. Used to perform consistent read-modify-write updates. If not set, a blind "overwrite" update happens.

fields.name: labels
fields.description: Optional. The labels with user-defined metadata to organize your FeatureOnlineStore. Label keys and values can be no longer than 64 characters (Unicode codepoints), can only contain lowercase letters, numeric characters, underscores and dashes. International characters are allowed. See https://goo.gl/xmQnxf for more information on and examples of labels. No more than 64 user labels can be associated with one FeatureOnlineStore(System labels are excluded)." System reserved label keys are prefixed with "aiplatform.googleapis.com/" and are immutable.

fields.name: satisfiesPzs
fields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

fields.name: satisfiesPzi
fields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

fields.name: enableDirectBigtableAccess
fields.description: Optional. It true, enable direct access to the Bigtable instance.

fields.name: zone
fields.description: Optional. The zone where the underlying Bigtable cluster for the primary Bigtable instance will be provisioned. Only the zone must be provided. For example, only "us-central1-a" should be provided.

fields.name: minNodeCount
fields.description: Required. The minimum number of nodes to scale down to. Must be greater than or equal to 1.

fields.name: maxNodeCount
fields.description: Required. The maximum number of nodes to scale up to. Must be greater than or equal to min_node_count, and less than or equal to 10 times of 'min_node_count'.

fields.name: cpuUtilizationTarget
fields.description: Optional. A percentage of the cluster's CPU capacity. Can be from 10% to 80%. When a cluster's CPU utilization exceeds the target that you have set, Bigtable immediately adds nodes to the cluster. When CPU utilization is substantially lower than the target, Bigtable removes nodes. If not set will default to 50%.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1FeatureOnlineStoreBigtableBigtableMetadata
types.description: Metadata of the Bigtable instance. This is used by direct read access to the Bigtable in tenant project.

fields.name: tenantProjectId
fields.description: Tenant project ID.

fields.name: instanceId
fields.description: The Cloud Bigtable instance id.

fields.name: tableId
fields.description: The Cloud Bigtable table id.

types.name: query_aiplatform_projects_locations_featureOnlineStores_list_featureOnlineStores_items_state
types.description: Output only. State of the featureOnlineStore.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1FeatureOnlineStoreDedicatedServingEndpoint
types.description: The dedicated serving endpoint for this FeatureOnlineStore. Only need to set when you choose Optimized storage type. Public endpoint is provisioned by default.

fields.name: publicEndpointDomainName
fields.description: Output only. This field will be populated with the domain name to use for this FeatureOnlineStore

fields.name: serviceAttachment
fields.description: Output only. The name of the service attachment resource. Populated if private service connect is enabled and after FeatureViewSync is created.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1PrivateServiceConnectConfig
types.description: Represents configuration for private service connect.

fields.description: Required. If true, expose the IndexEndpoint via private service connect.

fields.name: projectAllowlist
fields.description: A list of Projects from which the forwarding rule will target the service attachment.

fields.name: pscAutomationConfigs
fields.description: Optional. List of projects and networks where the PSC endpoints will be created. This field is used by Online Inference(Prediction) only.

fields.name: serviceAttachment
fields.description: Output only. The name of the generated service attachment resource. This is only populated if the endpoint is deployed with PrivateServiceConnect.

fields.name: ipAddress
fields.description: Output only. IP address rule created by the PSC service automation.

fields.name: forwardingRule
fields.description: Output only. Forwarding rule created by the PSC service automation.

fields.name: errorMessage

Label keys and values can be no longer than 64 characters (Unicode codepoints), can only contain lowercase letters, numeric characters, underscores and dashes. International characters are allowed. See https://goo.gl/xmQnxf for more information on and examples of labels. No more than 64 user labels can be associated with one FeatureOnlineStore(System labels are excluded)." System reserved label keys are prefixed with "aiplatform.googleapis.com/" and are immutable.

fields.name: serviceAccountEmail
fields.description: Output only. A Service Account unique to this FeatureView. The role bigquery.dataViewer should be granted to this service account to allow Vertex AI Feature Store to sync data to the online store.

fields.name: projectNumber
fields.description: Optional. The project number of the parent project of the Feature Groups.

types.description: Features belonging to a single feature group that will be synced to Online Store.

fields.description: Required. Identifier of the feature group.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1FeatureViewVertexRagSource
types.description: A Vertex Rag source for features that need to be synced to Online Store.

fields.name: uri
fields.description: Required. The BigQuery view/table URI that will be materialized on each manual sync trigger. The table/view is expected to have the following columns and types at least: - `corpus_id` (STRING, NULLABLE/REQUIRED) - `file_id` (STRING, NULLABLE/REQUIRED) - `chunk_id` (STRING, NULLABLE/REQUIRED) - `chunk_data_type` (STRING, NULLABLE/REQUIRED) - `chunk_data` (STRING, NULLABLE/REQUIRED) - `embeddings` (FLOAT, REPEATED) - `file_original_uri` (STRING, NULLABLE/REQUIRED)

fields.name: ragCorpusId
fields.description: Optional. The RAG corpus id corresponding to this FeatureView.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1FeatureViewSyncConfig
types.description: Configuration for Sync. Only one option is set.

fields.name: cron
fields.description: Cron schedule (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cron) to launch scheduled runs. To explicitly set a timezone to the cron tab, apply a prefix in the cron tab: "CRON_TZ=${IANA_TIME_ZONE}" or "TZ=${IANA_TIME_ZONE}". The ${IANA_TIME_ZONE} may only be a valid string from IANA time zone database. For example, "CRON_TZ=America/New_York 1 * * * *", or "TZ=America/New_York 1 * * * *".

fields.name: continuous
fields.description: Optional. If true, syncs the FeatureView in a continuous manner to Online Store.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1FeatureViewIndexConfig
types.description: Configuration for vector indexing.

fields.name: bruteForceConfig
fields.description: Configuration options for using brute force search.

fields.name: embeddingColumn
fields.description: Optional. Column of embedding. This column contains the source data to create index for vector search. embedding_column must be set when using vector search.

fields.name: filterColumns
fields.description: Optional. Columns of features that're used to filter vector search results.

fields.name: crowdingColumn
fields.description: Optional. Column of crowding. This column contains crowding attribute which is a constraint on a neighbor list produced by FeatureOnlineStoreService.SearchNearestEntities to diversify search results. If NearestNeighborQuery.per_crowding_attribute_neighbor_count is set to K in SearchNearestEntitiesRequest, it's guaranteed that no more than K entities of the same crowding attribute are returned in the response.

fields.name: embeddingDimension
fields.description: Optional. The number of dimensions of the input embedding.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1FeatureViewIndexConfigTreeAHConfig
types.description: Configuration options for the tree-AH algorithm.

fields.name: leafNodeEmbeddingCount
fields.description: Optional. Number of embeddings on each leaf node. The default value is 1000 if not set.

types.name: query_aiplatform_projects_locations_featureOnlineStores_featureViews_list_featureViews_items_indexConfig_distanceMeasureType
types.description: Optional. The distance measure used in nearest neighbor search.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1FeatureViewOptimizedConfig
types.description: Configuration for FeatureViews created in Optimized FeatureOnlineStore.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1AutomaticResources
types.description: A description of resources that to large degree are decided by Vertex AI, and require only a modest additional configuration. Each Model supporting these resources documents its specific guidelines.

fields.name: minReplicaCount
fields.description: Immutable. The minimum number of replicas that will be always deployed on. If traffic against it increases, it may dynamically be deployed onto more replicas up to max_replica_count, and as traffic decreases, some of these extra replicas may be freed. If the requested value is too large, the deployment will error.

fields.name: maxReplicaCount
fields.description: Immutable. The maximum number of replicas that may be deployed on when the traffic against it increases. If the requested value is too large, the deployment will error, but if deployment succeeds then the ability to scale to that many replicas is guaranteed (barring service outages). If traffic increases beyond what its replicas at maximum may handle, a portion of the traffic will be dropped. If this value is not provided, a no upper bound for scaling under heavy traffic will be assume, though Vertex AI may be unable to scale beyond certain replica number.

types.name: query_aiplatform_projects_locations_featureOnlineStores_featureViews_list_featureViews_items_serviceAgentType
types.description: Optional. Service agent type used during data sync. By default, the Vertex AI Service Agent is used. When using an IAM Policy to isolate this FeatureView within a project, a separate service account should be provisioned by setting this field to `SERVICE_AGENT_TYPE_FEATURE_VIEW`. This will generate a separate service account to access the BigQuery source table.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1FeatureViewBigtableMetadata
types.description: Metadata for the Cloud Bigtable that supports directly interacting Bigtable instances.

fields.name: readAppProfile
fields.description: Output only. The Bigtable App Profile to use for reading from Bigtable.

types.description: FeatureViewSync is a representation of sync operation which copies data from data source to Feature View in Online Store.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Identifier. Name of the FeatureViewSync. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/featureOnlineStores/{feature_online_store}/featureViews/{feature_view}/featureViewSyncs/{feature_view_sync}`

fields.name: createTime
fields.description: Output only. Time when this FeatureViewSync is created. Creation of a FeatureViewSync means that the job is pending / waiting for sufficient resources but may not have started the actual data transfer yet.

fields.name: satisfiesPzs
fields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

fields.name: satisfiesPzi
fields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

types.name: GoogleTypeInterval
types.description: Represents a time interval, encoded as a Timestamp start (inclusive) and a Timestamp end (exclusive). The start must be less than or equal to the end. When the start equals the end, the interval is empty (matches no time). When both start and end are unspecified, the interval matches any time.

fields.name: startTime
fields.description: Optional. Inclusive start of the interval. If specified, a Timestamp matching this interval will have to be the same or after the start.

fields.name: endTime
fields.description: Optional. Exclusive end of the interval. If specified, a Timestamp matching this interval will have to be before the end.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1FeatureViewSyncSyncSummary
types.description: Summary from the Sync job. For continuous syncs, the summary is updated periodically. For batch syncs, it gets updated on completion of the sync.

fields.name: rowSynced
fields.description: Output only. Total number of rows synced.

fields.name: totalSlot
fields.description: Output only. BigQuery slot milliseconds consumed for the sync job.

fields.name: systemWatermarkTime
fields.description: Lower bound of the system time watermark for the sync job. This is only set for continuously syncing feature views.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1FeatureGroupBigQuery
types.description: Input source type for BigQuery Tables and Views.

fields.name: entityIdColumns
fields.description: Optional. Columns to construct entity_id / row keys. If not provided defaults to `entity_id`.

fields.name: staticDataSource
fields.description: Optional. Set if the data source is not a time-series.

fields.name: dense
fields.description: Optional. If set, all feature values will be fetched from a single row per unique entityId including nulls. If not set, will collapse all rows for each unique entityId into a singe row with any non-null values if present, if no non-null values are present will sync null. ex: If source has schema `(entity_id, feature_timestamp, f0, f1)` and the following rows: `(e1, 2020-01-01T10:00:00.123Z, 10, 15)` `(e1, 2020-02-01T10:00:00.123Z, 20, null)` If dense is set, `(e1, 20, null)` is synced to online stores. If dense is not set, `(e1, 20, 15)` is synced to online stores.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1BigQuerySource

fields.name: inputUri
fields.description: Required. BigQuery URI to a table, up to 2000 characters long. Accepted forms: * BigQuery path. For example: `bq://projectId.bqDatasetId.bqTableId`.

fields.name: timestampColumn
fields.description: Optional. Column hosting timestamp values for a time-series source. Will be used to determine the latest `feature_values` for each entity. Optional. If not provided, column named `feature_timestamp` of type `TIMESTAMP` will be used.

When using an IAM Policy to isolate this FeatureGroup within a project, a separate service account should be provisioned by setting this field to `SERVICE_AGENT_TYPE_FEATURE_GROUP`. This will generate a separate service account to access the BigQuery source table.

fields.name: payloadSchemaUri
fields.description: Required. Google Cloud Storage URI points to a YAML file describing payload. The schema is defined as an [OpenAPI 3.0.2 Schema Object](https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/main/versions/3.0.2.md#schemaObject). The schema files that can be used here are found in gs://google-cloud-aiplatform/schema/dataset/annotation/, note that the chosen schema must be consistent with the parent Dataset's metadata.

fields.name: payload
fields.description: Required. The schema of the payload can be found in payload_schema.

fields.name: createTime
fields.description: Output only. Timestamp when this Annotation was created.

fields.name: updateTime
fields.description: Output only. Timestamp when this Annotation was last updated.

fields.name: etag
fields.description: Optional. Used to perform consistent read-modify-write updates. If not set, a blind "overwrite" update happens.

fields.description: Optional. The labels with user-defined metadata to organize your Annotations. Label keys and values can be no longer than 64 characters (Unicode codepoints), can only contain lowercase letters, numeric characters, underscores and dashes. International characters are allowed. No more than 64 user labels can be associated with one Annotation(System labels are excluded). See https://goo.gl/xmQnxf for more information and examples of labels. System reserved label keys are prefixed with "aiplatform.googleapis.com/" and are immutable. Following system labels exist for each Annotation: * "aiplatform.googleapis.com/annotation_set_name": optional, name of the UI's annotation set this Annotation belongs to. If not set, the Annotation is not visible in the UI. * "aiplatform.googleapis.com/payload_schema": output only, its value is the payload_schema's title.

It can contain one of the following values: * service-{project}@gcp-sa-aiplatform-re.googleapis.com (for SERVICE_AGENT identity type) * {name}@{project}.gserviceaccount.com (for SERVICE_ACCOUNT identity type) * agents.global.{org}.system.id.goog/resources/aiplatform/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/reasoningEngines/{reasoning_engine} (for AGENT_IDENTITY identity type)

fields.name: version
fields.description: Optional. The version of Python to use. Support version includes 3.9, 3.10, 3.11, 3.12, 3.13, 3.14. If not specified, default value is 3.10.

fields.name: entrypointModule
fields.description: Optional. The Python module to load as the entrypoint, specified as a fully qualified module name. For example: path.to.agent. If not specified, defaults to "agent". The project root will be added to Python sys.path, allowing imports to be specified relative to the root. This field should not be set if the source is `agent_config_source`.

fields.name: entrypointObject
fields.description: Optional. The name of the callable object within the `entrypoint_module` to use as the application If not specified, defaults to "root_agent". This field should not be set if the source is `agent_config_source`.

fields.name: requirementsFile
fields.description: Optional. The path to the requirements file, relative to the source root. If not specified, defaults to "requirements.txt".

fields.description: Optional. Environment variables to be set with the Reasoning Engine deployment. The environment variables can be updated through the UpdateReasoningEngine API.

fields.name: secretEnv
fields.description: Optional. Environment variables where the value is a secret in Cloud Secret Manager. To use this feature, add 'Secret Manager Secret Accessor' role (roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor) to AI Platform Reasoning Engine Service Agent.

fields.name: minInstances
fields.description: Optional. The minimum number of application instances that will be kept running at all times. Defaults to 1. Range: [0, 10].

fields.name: maxInstances
fields.description: Optional. The maximum number of application instances that can be launched to handle increased traffic. Defaults to 100. Range: [1, 1000]. If VPC-SC or PSC-I is enabled, the acceptable range is [1, 100].

fields.name: resourceLimits
fields.description: Optional. Resource limits for each container. Only 'cpu' and 'memory' keys are supported. Defaults to {"cpu": "4", "memory": "4Gi"}. * The only supported values for CPU are '1', '2', '4', '6' and '8'. For more information, go to https://cloud.google.com/run/docs/configuring/cpu. * The only supported values for memory are '1Gi', '2Gi', ... '32 Gi'. * For required cpu on different memory values, go to https://cloud.google.com/run/docs/configuring/memory-limits

fields.name: containerConcurrency
fields.description: Optional. Concurrency for each container and agent server. Recommended value: 2 * cpu + 1. Defaults to 9.

To specify this field, you must have already [created a network attachment] (https://cloud.google.com/vpc/docs/create-manage-network-attachments#create-network-attachments). This field is only used for resources using PSC-I.

fields.name: dnsPeeringConfigs
fields.description: Optional. DNS peering configurations. When specified, Vertex AI will attempt to configure DNS peering zones in the tenant project VPC to resolve the specified domains using the target network's Cloud DNS. The user must grant the dns.peer role to the Vertex AI Service Agent on the target project.

fields.name: targetNetwork

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1Content
types.description: The structured data content of a message. A Content message contains a `role` field, which indicates the producer of the content, and a `parts` field, which contains the multi-part data of the message.

fields.name: role
fields.description: Optional. The producer of the content. Must be either 'user' or 'model'. If not set, the service will default to 'user'.

fields.name: parts
fields.description: Required. A list of Part objects that make up a single message. Parts of a message can have different MIME types. A Content message must have at least one Part.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1Part
types.description: A datatype containing media that is part of a multi-part Content message. A `Part` consists of data which has an associated datatype. A `Part` can only contain one of the accepted types in `Part.data`. For media types that are not text, `Part` must have a fixed IANA MIME type identifying the type and subtype of the media if `inline_data` or `file_data` field is filled with raw bytes.

fields.name: text
fields.description: Optional. The text content of the part. When sent from the VSCode Gemini Code Assist extension, references to @mentioned items will be converted to markdown boldface text. For example `@my-repo` will be converted to and sent as `**my-repo**` by the IDE agent.

fields.name: thought
fields.description: Optional. Indicates whether the `part` represents the model's thought process or reasoning.

fields.name: thoughtSignature
fields.description: Optional. An opaque signature for the thought so it can be reused in subsequent requests.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1Blob
types.description: A content blob. A Blob contains data of a specific media type. It is used to represent images, audio, and video.

fields.name: mimeType
fields.description: Required. The IANA standard MIME type of the source data.

fields.name: data
fields.description: Required. The raw bytes of the data.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: Optional. The display name of the blob. Used to provide a label or filename to distinguish blobs. This field is only returned in `PromptMessage` for prompt management. It is used in the Gemini calls only when server-side tools (`code_execution`, `google_search`, and `url_context`) are enabled.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1FileData
types.description: URI-based data. A FileData message contains a URI pointing to data of a specific media type. It is used to represent images, audio, and video stored in Google Cloud Storage.

fields.name: mimeType
fields.description: Required. The IANA standard MIME type of the source data.

fields.name: fileUri
fields.description: Required. The URI of the file in Google Cloud Storage.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: Optional. The display name of the file. Used to provide a label or filename to distinguish files. This field is only returned in `PromptMessage` for prompt management. It is used in the Gemini calls only when server side tools (`code_execution`, `google_search`, and `url_context`) are enabled.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1FunctionCall
types.description: A predicted FunctionCall returned from the model that contains a string representing the FunctionDeclaration.name and a structured JSON object containing the parameters and their values.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Optional. The name of the function to call. Matches FunctionDeclaration.name.

fields.name: args
fields.description: Optional. The function parameters and values in JSON object format. See FunctionDeclaration.parameters for parameter details.

fields.name: partialArgs
fields.description: Optional. The partial argument value of the function call. If provided, represents the arguments/fields that are streamed incrementally.

fields.name: willContinue
fields.description: Optional. Whether this is the last part of the FunctionCall. If true, another partial message for the current FunctionCall is expected to follow.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1PartialArg
types.description: Partial argument value of the function call.

fields.name: numberValue
fields.description: Optional. Represents a double value.

fields.name: stringValue
fields.description: Optional. Represents a string value.

fields.name: boolValue
fields.description: Optional. Represents a boolean value.

fields.name: jsonPath
fields.description: Required. A JSON Path (RFC 9535) to the argument being streamed. https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9535. e.g. "$.foo.bar[0].data".

fields.name: willContinue
fields.description: Optional. Whether this is not the last part of the same json_path. If true, another PartialArg message for the current json_path is expected to follow.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1FunctionResponse
types.description: The result output from a FunctionCall that contains a string representing the FunctionDeclaration.name and a structured JSON object containing any output from the function is used as context to the model. This should contain the result of a `FunctionCall` made based on model prediction.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Required. The name of the function to call. Matches FunctionDeclaration.name and FunctionCall.name.

fields.name: response
fields.description: Required. The function response in JSON object format. Use "output" key to specify function output and "error" key to specify error details (if any). If "output" and "error" keys are not specified, then whole "response" is treated as function output.

fields.name: parts
fields.description: Optional. Ordered `Parts` that constitute a function response. Parts may have different IANA MIME types.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1FunctionResponsePart
types.description: A datatype containing media that is part of a `FunctionResponse` message. A `FunctionResponsePart` consists of data which has an associated datatype. A `FunctionResponsePart` can only contain one of the accepted types in `FunctionResponsePart.data`. A `FunctionResponsePart` must have a fixed IANA MIME type identifying the type and subtype of the media if the `inline_data` field is filled with raw bytes.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1FunctionResponseBlob
types.description: Raw media bytes for function response. Text should not be sent as raw bytes, use the 'text' field.

fields.name: mimeType
fields.description: Required. The IANA standard MIME type of the source data.

fields.name: data
fields.description: Required. Raw bytes.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: Optional. Display name of the blob. Used to provide a label or filename to distinguish blobs. This field is only returned in PromptMessage for prompt management. It is currently used in the Gemini GenerateContent calls only when server side tools (code_execution, google_search, and url_context) are enabled.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1FunctionResponseFileData
types.description: URI based data for function response.

fields.name: mimeType
fields.description: Required. The IANA standard MIME type of the source data.

fields.name: fileUri
fields.description: Required. URI.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: Optional. Display name of the file data. Used to provide a label or filename to distinguish file datas. This field is only returned in PromptMessage for prompt management. It is currently used in the Gemini GenerateContent calls only when server side tools (code_execution, google_search, and url_context) are enabled.

fields.name: author

fields.name: invocationId

fields.name: timestamp

fields.name: errorCode
fields.description: Optional. Error code if the response is an error. Code varies by model.

fields.name: errorMessage
fields.description: Optional. Error message if the response is an error.

fields.name: rawEvent
fields.description: Optional. Weakly typed raw event data in proto struct format.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1EventActions
types.description: Actions are parts of events that are executed by the agent.

fields.name: skipSummarization
fields.description: Optional. If true, it won't call model to summarize function response. Only used for function_response event.

fields.name: stateDelta
fields.description: Optional. Indicates that the event is updating the state with the given delta.

fields.name: artifactDelta
fields.description: Optional. Indicates that the event is updating an artifact. key is the filename, value is the version.

fields.name: escalate
fields.description: Optional. The agent is escalating to a higher level agent.

fields.name: requestedAuthConfigs
fields.description: Optional. Will only be set by a tool response indicating tool request euc. Struct key is the function call id since one function call response (from model) could correspond to multiple function calls. Struct value is the required auth config, which can be another struct.

fields.name: transferAgent
fields.description: Optional. If set, the event transfers to the specified agent.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1EventMetadata
types.description: Metadata relating to a LLM response event.

fields.name: partial
fields.description: Optional. Indicates whether the text content is part of a unfinished text stream. Only used for streaming mode and when the content is plain text.

fields.name: turnComplete
fields.description: Optional. Indicates whether the response from the model is complete. Only used for streaming mode.

fields.name: interrupted
fields.description: Optional. Flag indicating that LLM was interrupted when generating the content. Usually it's due to user interruption during a bidi streaming.

fields.name: longRunningToolIds
fields.description: Optional. Set of ids of the long running function calls. Agent client will know from this field about which function call is long running. Only valid for function call event.

fields.name: branch
fields.description: Optional. The branch of the event. The format is like agent_1.agent_2.agent_3, where agent_1 is the parent of agent_2, and agent_2 is the parent of agent_3. Branch is used when multiple child agents shouldn't see their siblings' conversation history.

fields.name: customMetadata
fields.description: The custom metadata of the LlmResponse.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GroundingMetadata
types.description: Information about the sources that support the content of a response. When grounding is enabled, the model returns citations for claims in the response. This object contains the retrieved sources.

fields.name: webSearchQueries
fields.description: Optional. The web search queries that were used to generate the content. This field is populated only when the grounding source is Google Search.

fields.name: imageSearchQueries
fields.description: Optional. The image search queries that were used to generate the content. This field is populated only when the grounding source is Google Search with the Image Search search_type enabled.

fields.name: groundingChunks
fields.description: A list of supporting references retrieved from the grounding source. This field is populated when the grounding source is Google Search, Vertex AI Search, or Google Maps.

fields.name: groundingSupports
fields.description: Optional. A list of grounding supports that connect the generated content to the grounding chunks. This field is populated when the grounding source is Google Search or Vertex AI Search.

fields.name: googleMapsWidgetContextToken
fields.description: Optional. Output only. A token that can be used to render a Google Maps widget with the contextual data. This field is populated only when the grounding source is Google Maps.

fields.name: sourceFlaggingUris
fields.description: Optional. Output only. A list of URIs that can be used to flag a place or review for inappropriate content. This field is populated only when the grounding source is Google Maps.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1SearchEntryPoint
types.description: An entry point for displaying Google Search results. A `SearchEntryPoint` is populated when the grounding source for a model's response is Google Search. It provides information that you can use to display the search results in your application.

fields.name: renderedContent
fields.description: Optional. An HTML snippet that can be embedded in a web page or an application's webview. This snippet displays a search result, including the title, URL, and a brief description of the search result.

fields.name: sdkBlob
fields.description: Optional. A base64-encoded JSON object that contains a list of search queries and their corresponding search URLs. This information can be used to build a custom search UI.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GroundingChunk
types.description: A piece of evidence that supports a claim made by the model. This is used to show a citation for a claim made by the model. When grounding is enabled, the model returns a `GroundingChunk` that contains a reference to the source of the information.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GroundingChunkWeb
types.description: A `Web` chunk is a piece of evidence that comes from a web page. It contains the URI of the web page, the title of the page, and the domain of the page. This is used to provide the user with a link to the source of the information.

fields.name: uri
fields.description: The URI of the web page that contains the evidence.

fields.name: title
fields.description: The title of the web page that contains the evidence.

fields.name: domain
fields.description: The domain of the web page that contains the evidence. This can be used to filter out low-quality sources.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GroundingChunkImage
types.description: An `Image` chunk is a piece of evidence that comes from an image search result. It contains the URI of the image search result and the URI of the image. This is used to provide the user with a link to the source of the information.

fields.name: sourceUri
fields.description: The URI of the image search result page.

fields.name: imageUri
fields.description: The URI of the image.

fields.name: title
fields.description: The title of the image search result page.

fields.name: domain
fields.description: The domain of the image search result page.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GroundingChunkRetrievedContext
types.description: Context retrieved from a data source to ground the model's response. This is used when a retrieval tool fetches information from a user-provided corpus or a public dataset.

fields.name: uri
fields.description: The URI of the retrieved data source.

fields.name: title
fields.description: The title of the retrieved data source.

fields.name: text
fields.description: The content of the retrieved data source.

fields.name: documentName
fields.description: Output only. The full resource name of the referenced Vertex AI Search document. This is used to identify the specific document that was retrieved. The format is `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/collections/{collection}/dataStores/{data_store}/branches/{branch}/documents/{document}`.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1RagChunk
types.description: A RagChunk includes the content of a chunk of a RagFile, and associated metadata.

fields.name: text
fields.description: The content of the chunk.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1RagChunkPageSpan
types.description: Represents where the chunk starts and ends in the document.

fields.name: firstPage
fields.description: Page where chunk starts in the document. Inclusive. 1-indexed.

fields.name: lastPage
fields.description: Page where chunk ends in the document. Inclusive. 1-indexed.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GroundingChunkMaps
types.description: A `Maps` chunk is a piece of evidence that comes from Google Maps, containing information about places or routes. This is used to provide the user with rich, location-based information.

fields.name: uri
fields.description: The URI of the place.

fields.name: title
fields.description: The title of the place.

fields.name: text
fields.description: The text of the place answer.

fields.name: placeId
fields.description: This Place's resource name, in `places/{place_id}` format. This can be used to look up the place in the Google Maps API.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GroundingChunkMapsPlaceAnswerSources
types.description: The sources that were used to generate the place answer. This includes review snippets and photos that were used to generate the answer, as well as URIs to flag content.

fields.name: reviewSnippets
fields.description: Snippets of reviews that were used to generate the answer.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GroundingChunkMapsPlaceAnswerSourcesReviewSnippet
types.description: A review snippet that is used to generate the answer.

fields.name: reviewId
fields.description: The ID of the review that is being referenced.

fields.name: googleMapsUri
fields.description: A link to show the review on Google Maps.

fields.name: title
fields.description: The title of the review.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GroundingChunkMapsRoute
types.description: Route information from Google Maps.

fields.name: distanceMeters
fields.description: The total distance of the route, in meters.

fields.name: duration
fields.description: The total duration of the route.

fields.name: encodedPolyline
fields.description: An encoded polyline of the route. See https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/utilities/polylinealgorithm

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GroundingSupport
types.description: A collection of supporting references for a segment or part of the model's response.

fields.name: groundingChunkIndices
fields.description: A list of indices into the `grounding_chunks` field of the `GroundingMetadata` message. These indices specify which grounding chunks support the claim made in the content segment. For example, if this field has the values `[1, 3]`, it means that `grounding_chunks[1]` and `grounding_chunks[3]` are the sources for the claim in the content segment.

fields.name: confidenceScores
fields.description: The confidence scores for the support references. This list is parallel to the `grounding_chunk_indices` list. A score is a value between 0.0 and 1.0, with a higher score indicating a higher confidence that the reference supports the claim. For Gemini 2.0 and before, this list has the same size as `grounding_chunk_indices`. For Gemini 2.5 and later, this list is empty and should be ignored.

fields.name: renderedParts
fields.description: Indices into the `rendered_parts` field of the `GroundingMetadata` message. These indices specify which rendered parts are associated with this support message.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1Segment
types.description: A segment of the content.

fields.name: partIndex
fields.description: Output only. The index of the `Part` object that this segment belongs to. This is useful for associating the segment with a specific part of the content.

fields.name: startIndex
fields.description: Output only. The start index of the segment in the `Part`, measured in bytes. This marks the beginning of the segment and is inclusive, meaning the byte at this index is the first byte of the segment.

fields.name: endIndex
fields.description: Output only. The end index of the segment in the `Part`, measured in bytes. This marks the end of the segment and is exclusive, meaning the segment includes content up to, but not including, the byte at this index.

fields.name: text
fields.description: Output only. The text of the segment.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1RetrievalMetadata
types.description: Metadata related to the retrieval grounding source. This is part of the `GroundingMetadata` returned when grounding is enabled.

fields.name: googleSearchDynamicRetrievalScore
fields.description: Optional. A score indicating how likely it is that a Google Search query could help answer the prompt. The score is in the range of `[0, 1]`. A score of 1 means the model is confident that a search will be helpful, and 0 means it is not. This score is populated only when Google Search grounding and dynamic retrieval are enabled. The score is used to determine whether to trigger a search.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GroundingMetadataSourceFlaggingUri
types.description: A URI that can be used to flag a place or review for inappropriate content. This is populated only when the grounding source is Google Maps.

fields.name: sourceId
fields.description: The ID of the place or review.

fields.name: flagContentUri
fields.description: The URI that can be used to flag the content.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1Transcription
types.description: Audio transcription in Server Content.

fields.name: text
fields.description: Optional. Transcription text.

fields.name: finished
fields.description: Optional. The bool indicates the end of the transcription.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1DeploymentResourcePool
types.description: A description of resources that can be shared by multiple DeployedModels, whose underlying specification consists of a DedicatedResources.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Immutable. The resource name of the DeploymentResourcePool. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/deploymentResourcePools/{deployment_resource_pool}`

fields.name: serviceAccount
fields.description: The service account that the DeploymentResourcePool's container(s) run as. Specify the email address of the service account. If this service account is not specified, the container(s) run as a service account that doesn't have access to the resource project. Users deploying the Models to this DeploymentResourcePool must have the `iam.serviceAccounts.actAs` permission on this service account.

fields.name: disableContainerLogging
fields.description: If the DeploymentResourcePool is deployed with custom-trained Models or AutoML Tabular Models, the container(s) of the DeploymentResourcePool will send `stderr` and `stdout` streams to Cloud Logging by default. Please note that the logs incur cost, which are subject to [Cloud Logging pricing](https://cloud.google.com/logging/pricing). User can disable container logging by setting this flag to true.

fields.name: createTime
fields.description: Output only. Timestamp when this DeploymentResourcePool was created.

fields.name: satisfiesPzs
fields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

fields.name: satisfiesPzi
fields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1DedicatedResources
types.description: A description of resources that are dedicated to a DeployedModel or DeployedIndex, and that need a higher degree of manual configuration.

fields.name: minReplicaCount
fields.description: Required. Immutable. The minimum number of machine replicas that will be always deployed on. This value must be greater than or equal to 1. If traffic increases, it may dynamically be deployed onto more replicas, and as traffic decreases, some of these extra replicas may be freed.

fields.name: maxReplicaCount
fields.description: Immutable. The maximum number of replicas that may be deployed on when the traffic against it increases. If the requested value is too large, the deployment will error, but if deployment succeeds then the ability to scale to that many replicas is guaranteed (barring service outages). If traffic increases beyond what its replicas at maximum may handle, a portion of the traffic will be dropped. If this value is not provided, will use min_replica_count as the default value. The value of this field impacts the charge against Vertex CPU and GPU quotas. Specifically, you will be charged for (max_replica_count * number of cores in the selected machine type) and (max_replica_count * number of GPUs per replica in the selected machine type).

fields.name: requiredReplicaCount
fields.description: Optional. Number of required available replicas for the deployment to succeed. This field is only needed when partial deployment/mutation is desired. If set, the deploy/mutate operation will succeed once available_replica_count reaches required_replica_count, and the rest of the replicas will be retried. If not set, the default required_replica_count will be min_replica_count.

fields.name: autoscalingMetricSpecs
fields.description: Immutable. The metric specifications that overrides a resource utilization metric (CPU utilization, accelerator's duty cycle, and so on) target value (default to 60 if not set). At most one entry is allowed per metric. If machine_spec.accelerator_count is above 0, the autoscaling will be based on both CPU utilization and accelerator's duty cycle metrics and scale up when either metrics exceeds its target value while scale down if both metrics are under their target value. The default target value is 60 for both metrics. If machine_spec.accelerator_count is 0, the autoscaling will be based on CPU utilization metric only with default target value 60 if not explicitly set. For example, in the case of Online Prediction, if you want to override target CPU utilization to 80, you should set autoscaling_metric_specs.metric_name to `aiplatform.googleapis.com/prediction/online/cpu/utilization` and autoscaling_metric_specs.target to `80`.

fields.name: spot
fields.description: Optional. If true, schedule the deployment workload on [spot VMs](https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/spot-vms).

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1AutoscalingMetricSpec
types.description: The metric specification that defines the target resource utilization (CPU utilization, accelerator's duty cycle, and so on) for calculating the desired replica count.

fields.name: metricName
fields.description: Required. The resource metric name. Supported metrics: * For Online Prediction: * `aiplatform.googleapis.com/prediction/online/accelerator/duty_cycle` * `aiplatform.googleapis.com/prediction/online/cpu/utilization` * `aiplatform.googleapis.com/prediction/online/request_count` * `pubsub.googleapis.com/subscription/num_undelivered_messages` * `prometheus.googleapis.com/vertex_dcgm_fi_dev_gpu_util` * `prometheus.googleapis.com/vertex_vllm_gpu_cache_usage_perc` * `prometheus.googleapis.com/vertex_vllm_num_requests_waiting`

fields.name: target
fields.description: The target resource utilization in percentage (1% - 100%) for the given metric; once the real usage deviates from the target by a certain percentage, the machine replicas change. The default value is 60 (representing 60%) if not provided.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1QueryDeployedModelsResponse

fields.name: deployedModels

fields.name: nextPageToken
fields.description: A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page. If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

fields.name: totalDeployedModelCount

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1DeployedModel
types.description: A deployment of a Model. Endpoints contain one or more DeployedModels.

fields.name: sharedResources
fields.description: The resource name of the shared DeploymentResourcePool to deploy on. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/deploymentResourcePools/{deployment_resource_pool}`

fields.name: id
fields.description: Immutable. The ID of the DeployedModel. If not provided upon deployment, Vertex AI will generate a value for this ID. This value should be 1-10 characters, and valid characters are `/[0-9]/`.

fields.name: model
fields.description: The resource name of the Model that this is the deployment of. Note that the Model may be in a different location than the DeployedModel's Endpoint. The resource name may contain version id or version alias to specify the version. Example: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/models/{model}@2` or `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/models/{model}@golden` if no version is specified, the default version will be deployed.

fields.name: gdcConnectedModel
fields.description: GDC pretrained / Gemini model name. The model name is a plain model name, e.g. gemini-1.5-flash-002.

fields.name: modelVersionId
fields.description: Output only. The version ID of the model that is deployed.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: The display name of the DeployedModel. If not provided upon creation, the Model's display_name is used.

fields.name: createTime
fields.description: Output only. Timestamp when the DeployedModel was created.

fields.name: disableExplanations
fields.description: If true, deploy the model without explainable feature, regardless the existence of Model.explanation_spec or explanation_spec.

fields.name: serviceAccount
fields.description: The service account that the DeployedModel's container runs as. Specify the email address of the service account. If this service account is not specified, the container runs as a service account that doesn't have access to the resource project. Users deploying the Model must have the `iam.serviceAccounts.actAs` permission on this service account.

fields.name: disableContainerLogging
fields.description: For custom-trained Models and AutoML Tabular Models, the container of the DeployedModel instances will send `stderr` and `stdout` streams to Cloud Logging by default. Please note that the logs incur cost, which are subject to [Cloud Logging pricing](https://cloud.google.com/logging/pricing). User can disable container logging by setting this flag to true.

fields.name: enableAccessLogging
fields.description: If true, online prediction access logs are sent to Cloud Logging. These logs are like standard server access logs, containing information like timestamp and latency for each prediction request. Note that logs may incur a cost, especially if your project receives prediction requests at a high queries per second rate (QPS). Estimate your costs before enabling this option.

fields.name: systemLabels
fields.description: System labels to apply to Model Garden deployments. System labels are managed by Google for internal use only.

fields.name: checkpointId
fields.description: The checkpoint id of the model.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1PrivateEndpoints
types.description: PrivateEndpoints proto is used to provide paths for users to send requests privately. To send request via private service access, use predict_http_uri, explain_http_uri or health_http_uri. To send request via private service connect, use service_attachment.

fields.name: fastTryoutEnabled
fields.description: If true, enable fast tryout feature for this deployed model.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1DeployedModelStatus
types.description: Runtime status of the deployed model.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Output only. The latest deployed model's status message (if any).

fields.name: lastUpdateTime
fields.description: Output only. The time at which the status was last updated.

fields.name: availableReplicaCount
fields.description: Output only. The number of available replicas of the deployed model.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1SpeculativeDecodingSpec
types.description: Configuration for Speculative Decoding.

fields.name: speculativeTokenCount
fields.description: The number of speculative tokens to generate at each step.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1SpeculativeDecodingSpecDraftModelSpeculation
types.description: Draft model speculation works by using the smaller model to generate candidate tokens for speculative decoding.

fields.name: draftModel
fields.description: Required. The resource name of the draft model.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1SpeculativeDecodingSpecNgramSpeculation
types.description: N-Gram speculation works by trying to find matching tokens in the previous prompt sequence and use those as speculation for generating new tokens.

fields.name: ngramSize
fields.description: The number of last N input tokens used as ngram to search/match against the previous prompt sequence. This is equal to the N in N-Gram. The default value is 3 if not specified.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ListEndpointsResponse
types.description: Response message for EndpointService.ListEndpoints.

fields.name: endpoints
fields.description: List of Endpoints in the requested page.

fields.name: nextPageToken
fields.description: A token to retrieve the next page of results. Pass to ListEndpointsRequest.page_token to obtain that page.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1Endpoint
types.description: Models are deployed into it, and afterwards Endpoint is called to obtain predictions and explanations.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Identifier. The resource name of the Endpoint.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: Required. The display name of the Endpoint. The name can be up to 128 characters long and can consist of any UTF-8 characters.

fields.name: description
fields.description: The description of the Endpoint.

fields.name: deployedModels
fields.description: Output only. The models deployed in this Endpoint. To add or remove DeployedModels use EndpointService.DeployModel and EndpointService.UndeployModel respectively.

fields.name: trafficSplit
fields.description: A map from a DeployedModel's ID to the percentage of this Endpoint's traffic that should be forwarded to that DeployedModel. If a DeployedModel's ID is not listed in this map, then it receives no traffic. The traffic percentage values must add up to 100, or map must be empty if the Endpoint is to not accept any traffic at a moment.

fields.name: etag
fields.description: Used to perform consistent read-modify-write updates. If not set, a blind "overwrite" update happens.

fields.name: labels
fields.description: The labels with user-defined metadata to organize your Endpoints. Label keys and values can be no longer than 64 characters (Unicode codepoints), can only contain lowercase letters, numeric characters, underscores and dashes. International characters are allowed. See https://goo.gl/xmQnxf for more information and examples of labels.

fields.name: createTime
fields.description: Output only. Timestamp when this Endpoint was created.

fields.name: updateTime
fields.description: Output only. Timestamp when this Endpoint was last updated.

fields.name: network
fields.description: Optional. The full name of the Google Compute Engine [network](https://cloud.google.com//compute/docs/networks-and-firewalls#networks) to which the Endpoint should be peered. Private services access must already be configured for the network. If left unspecified, the Endpoint is not peered with any network. Only one of the fields, network or enable_private_service_connect, can be set. [Format](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/reference/rest/v1/networks/insert): `projects/{project}/global/networks/{network}`. Where `{project}` is a project number, as in `12345`, and `{network}` is network name.

fields.name: enablePrivateServiceConnect
fields.description: Deprecated: If true, expose the Endpoint via private service connect. Only one of the fields, network or enable_private_service_connect, can be set.

fields.name: modelDeploymentMonitoringJob
fields.description: Output only. Resource name of the Model Monitoring job associated with this Endpoint if monitoring is enabled by JobService.CreateModelDeploymentMonitoringJob. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/modelDeploymentMonitoringJobs/{model_deployment_monitoring_job}`

fields.name: dedicatedEndpointEnabled
fields.description: If true, the endpoint will be exposed through a dedicated DNS [Endpoint.dedicated_endpoint_dns]. Your request to the dedicated DNS will be isolated from other users' traffic and will have better performance and reliability. Note: Once you enabled dedicated endpoint, you won't be able to send request to the shared DNS {region}-aiplatform.googleapis.com. The limitation will be removed soon.

fields.name: dedicatedEndpointDns
fields.description: Output only. DNS of the dedicated endpoint. Will only be populated if dedicated_endpoint_enabled is true. Depending on the features enabled, uid might be a random number or a string. For example, if fast_tryout is enabled, uid will be fasttryout. Format: `https://{endpoint_id}.{region}-{uid}.prediction.vertexai.goog`.

fields.name: satisfiesPzs
fields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

fields.name: satisfiesPzi
fields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1PredictRequestResponseLoggingConfig
types.description: Configuration for logging request-response to a BigQuery table.

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: If logging is enabled or not.

fields.name: samplingRate
fields.description: Percentage of requests to be logged, expressed as a fraction in range(0,1].

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1BigQueryDestination
types.description: The BigQuery location for the output content.

fields.name: outputUri
fields.description: Required. BigQuery URI to a project or table, up to 2000 characters long. When only the project is specified, the Dataset and Table is created. When the full table reference is specified, the Dataset must exist and table must not exist. Accepted forms: * BigQuery path. For example: `bq://projectId` or `bq://projectId.bqDatasetId` or `bq://projectId.bqDatasetId.bqTableId`.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GdcConfig
types.description: Google Distributed Cloud (GDC) config.

fields.name: zone
fields.description: GDC zone. A cluster will be designated for the Vertex AI workload in this zone.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ClientConnectionConfig
types.description: Configurations (e.g. inference timeout) that are applied on your endpoints.

fields.name: inferenceTimeout
fields.description: Customizable online prediction request timeout.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GenAiAdvancedFeaturesConfig
types.description: Configuration for GenAiAdvancedFeatures.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GenAiAdvancedFeaturesConfigRagConfig
types.description: Configuration for Retrieval Augmented Generation feature.

fields.name: enableRag
fields.description: If true, enable Retrieval Augmented Generation in ChatCompletion request. Once enabled, the endpoint will be identified as GenAI endpoint and Arthedain router will be used.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ListEvaluationRunsResponse
types.description: Response message for EvaluationManagementService.ListEvaluationRuns.

fields.name: evaluationRuns
fields.description: List of EvaluationRuns in the requested page.

fields.name: nextPageToken
fields.description: A token to retrieve the next page of results.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1EvaluationRun
types.description: EvaluationRun is a resource that represents a single evaluation run, which includes a set of prompts, model responses, evaluation configuration and the resulting metrics.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Identifier. The resource name of the EvaluationRun. This is a unique identifier. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/evaluationRuns/{evaluation_run}`

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: Required. The display name of the Evaluation Run.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Optional. Metadata about the evaluation run, can be used by the caller to store additional tracking information about the evaluation run.

fields.name: labels
fields.description: Optional. Labels for the evaluation run.

fields.name: createTime
fields.description: Output only. Time when the evaluation run was created.

fields.name: completionTime
fields.description: Output only. Time when the evaluation run was completed.

fields.name: evaluationSetSnapshot
fields.description: Output only. The specific evaluation set of the evaluation run. For runs with an evaluation set input, this will be that same set. For runs with BigQuery input, it's the sampled BigQuery dataset.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1EvaluationRunDataSource
types.description: The data source for the evaluation run.

fields.name: evaluationSet
fields.description: The EvaluationSet resource name. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/evaluationSets/{evaluation_set}`

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1BigQueryRequestSet
types.description: The request set for the evaluation run.

fields.name: uri
fields.description: Required. The URI of a BigQuery table. e.g. bq://projectId.bqDatasetId.bqTableId

fields.name: promptColumn
fields.description: Optional. The name of the column that contains the requests to evaluate. This will be in evaluation_item.EvalPrompt format.

fields.name: rubricsColumn
fields.description: Optional. The name of the column that contains the rubrics. This is in evaluation_rubric.RubricGroup format.

fields.name: candidateResponseColumns
fields.description: Optional. Map of candidate name to candidate response column name. The column will be in evaluation_item.CandidateResponse format.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1BigQueryRequestSetSamplingConfig
types.description: The sampling config.

fields.name: samplingCount
fields.description: Optional. The total number of logged data to import. If available data is less than the sampling count, all data will be imported. Default is 100.

fields.name: samplingDuration
fields.description: Optional. How long to wait before sampling data from the BigQuery table. If not specified, defaults to 0.

types.name: query_aiplatform_projects_locations_evaluationRuns_list_evaluationRuns_items_dataSource_bigqueryRequestSet_samplingConfig_samplingMethod
types.description: Optional. The sampling method to use.

types.name: query_aiplatform_projects_locations_evaluationRuns_list_evaluationRuns_items_inferenceConfigs
types.description: Optional. The candidate to inference config map for the evaluation run. The candidate can be up to 128 characters long and can consist of any UTF-8 characters.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1EvaluationRunInferenceConfig
types.description: Defines the configuration for a candidate model or agent being evaluated. `InferenceConfig` encapsulates all the necessary information to invoke or scrape the candidate during the evaluation run. This includes direct model inference parameters, agent execution settings, and multi-turn scraping configurations (such as user simulators). It serves as the primary representation of the candidate across different stages of the evaluation process.

fields.name: model
fields.description: Optional. The fully qualified name of the publisher model or endpoint to use. Anthropic and Llama third-party models are also supported through Model Garden. Publisher model format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/publishers/*/models/*` Third-party model formats: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/publishers/anthropic/models/{model}` or `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/publishers/llama/models/{model}` Endpoint format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/endpoints/{endpoint}`

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GenerationConfig
types.description: Configuration for content generation. This message contains all the parameters that control how the model generates content. It allows you to influence the randomness, length, and structure of the output.

fields.name: temperature
fields.description: Optional. Controls the randomness of the output. A higher temperature results in more creative and diverse responses, while a lower temperature makes the output more predictable and focused. The valid range is (0.0, 2.0].

fields.name: topP
fields.description: Optional. Specifies the nucleus sampling threshold. The model considers only the smallest set of tokens whose cumulative probability is at least `top_p`. This helps generate more diverse and less repetitive responses. For example, a `top_p` of 0.9 means the model considers tokens until the cumulative probability of the tokens to select from reaches 0.9. It's recommended to adjust either temperature or `top_p`, but not both.

fields.name: topK
fields.description: Optional. Specifies the top-k sampling threshold. The model considers only the top k most probable tokens for the next token. This can be useful for generating more coherent and less random text. For example, a `top_k` of 40 means the model will choose the next word from the 40 most likely words.

fields.name: candidateCount
fields.description: Optional. The number of candidate responses to generate. A higher `candidate_count` can provide more options to choose from, but it also consumes more resources. This can be useful for generating a variety of responses and selecting the best one.

fields.name: maxOutputTokens
fields.description: Optional. The maximum number of tokens to generate in the response. A token is approximately four characters. The default value varies by model. This parameter can be used to control the length of the generated text and prevent overly long responses.

fields.name: stopSequences
fields.description: Optional. A list of character sequences that will stop the model from generating further tokens. If a stop sequence is generated, the output will end at that point. This is useful for controlling the length and structure of the output. For example, you can use ["\n", "###"] to stop generation at a new line or a specific marker.

fields.name: responseLogprobs
fields.description: Optional. If set to true, the log probabilities of the output tokens are returned. Log probabilities are the logarithm of the probability of a token appearing in the output. A higher log probability means the token is more likely to be generated. This can be useful for analyzing the model's confidence in its own output and for debugging.

fields.name: logprobs
fields.description: Optional. The number of top log probabilities to return for each token. This can be used to see which other tokens were considered likely candidates for a given position. A higher value will return more options, but it will also increase the size of the response.

fields.name: presencePenalty
fields.description: Optional. Penalizes tokens that have already appeared in the generated text. A positive value encourages the model to generate more diverse and less repetitive text. Valid values can range from [-2.0, 2.0].

fields.name: frequencyPenalty
fields.description: Optional. Penalizes tokens based on their frequency in the generated text. A positive value helps to reduce the repetition of words and phrases. Valid values can range from [-2.0, 2.0].

fields.name: seed
fields.description: Optional. A seed for the random number generator. By setting a seed, you can make the model's output mostly deterministic. For a given prompt and parameters (like temperature, top_p, etc.), the model will produce the same response every time. However, it's not a guaranteed absolute deterministic behavior. This is different from parameters like `temperature`, which control the *level* of randomness. `seed` ensures that the "random" choices the model makes are the same on every run, making it essential for testing and ensuring reproducible results.

fields.name: responseMimeType
fields.description: Optional. The IANA standard MIME type of the response. The model will generate output that conforms to this MIME type. Supported values include 'text/plain' (default) and 'application/json'. The model needs to be prompted to output the appropriate response type, otherwise the behavior is undefined.

fields.name: responseJsonSchema
fields.description: Optional. When this field is set, response_schema must be omitted and response_mime_type must be set to `application/json`.

fields.name: audioTimestamp
fields.description: Optional. If enabled, audio timestamps will be included in the request to the model. This can be useful for synchronizing audio with other modalities in the response.

fields.name: responseModalities
fields.description: Optional. The modalities of the response. The model will generate a response that includes all the specified modalities. For example, if this is set to `[TEXT, IMAGE]`, the response will include both text and an image.

fields.name: enableAffectiveDialog
fields.description: Optional. If enabled, the model will detect emotions and adapt its responses accordingly. For example, if the model detects that the user is frustrated, it may provide a more empathetic response.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GenerationConfigRoutingConfig
types.description: The configuration for routing the request to a specific model. This can be used to control which model is used for the generation, either automatically or by specifying a model name.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GenerationConfigRoutingConfigAutoRoutingMode
types.description: The configuration for automated routing. When automated routing is specified, the routing will be determined by the pretrained routing model and customer provided model routing preference.

types.name: query_aiplatform_projects_locations_evaluationRuns_list_evaluationRuns_items_inferenceConfigs_additionalProperties_generationConfig_routingConfig_autoMode_modelRoutingPreference
types.description: The model routing preference.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GenerationConfigRoutingConfigManualRoutingMode
types.description: The configuration for manual routing. When manual routing is specified, the model will be selected based on the model name provided.

fields.name: modelName
fields.description: The name of the model to use. Only public LLM models are accepted.

types.name: query_aiplatform_projects_locations_evaluationRuns_list_evaluationRuns_items_inferenceConfigs_additionalProperties_generationConfig_mediaResolution
types.description: Optional. The token resolution at which input media content is sampled. This is used to control the trade-off between the quality of the response and the number of tokens used to represent the media. A higher resolution allows the model to perceive more detail, which can lead to a more nuanced response, but it will also use more tokens. This does not affect the image dimensions sent to the model.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1SpeechConfig
types.description: Configuration for speech generation.

fields.name: languageCode
fields.description: Optional. The language code (ISO 639-1) for the speech synthesis.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1VoiceConfig
types.description: Configuration for a voice.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1PrebuiltVoiceConfig
types.description: Configuration for a prebuilt voice.

fields.name: voiceName
fields.description: The name of the prebuilt voice to use.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ReplicatedVoiceConfig
types.description: The configuration for the replicated voice to use.

fields.name: mimeType
fields.description: Optional. The mimetype of the voice sample. The only currently supported value is `audio/wav`. This represents 16-bit signed little-endian wav data, with a 24kHz sampling rate. `mime_type` will default to `audio/wav` if not set.

fields.name: voiceSampleAudio
fields.description: Optional. The sample of the custom voice.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1MultiSpeakerVoiceConfig
types.description: Configuration for a multi-speaker text-to-speech request.

fields.name: speakerVoiceConfigs
fields.description: Required. A list of configurations for the voices of the speakers. Exactly two speaker voice configurations must be provided.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1SpeakerVoiceConfig
types.description: Configuration for a single speaker in a multi-speaker setup.

fields.name: speaker
fields.description: Required. The name of the speaker. This should be the same as the speaker name used in the prompt.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GenerationConfigThinkingConfig
types.description: Configuration for the model's thinking features. "Thinking" is a process where the model breaks down a complex task into smaller, manageable steps. This allows the model to reason about the task, plan its approach, and execute the plan to generate a high-quality response.

fields.name: includeThoughts
fields.description: Optional. If true, the model will include its thoughts in the response. "Thoughts" are the intermediate steps the model takes to arrive at the final response. They can provide insights into the model's reasoning process and help with debugging. If this is true, thoughts are returned only when available.

fields.name: thinkingBudget
fields.description: Optional. The token budget for the model's thinking process. The model will make a best effort to stay within this budget. This can be used to control the trade-off between response quality and latency.

types.name: query_aiplatform_projects_locations_evaluationRuns_list_evaluationRuns_items_inferenceConfigs_additionalProperties_generationConfig_thinkingConfig_thinkingLevel
types.description: Optional. The number of thoughts tokens that the model should generate.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ImageConfig
types.description: Configuration for image generation. This message allows you to control various aspects of image generation, such as the output format, aspect ratio, and whether the model can generate images of people.

fields.name: aspectRatio
fields.description: Optional. The desired aspect ratio for the generated images. The following aspect ratios are supported: "1:1" "2:3", "3:2" "3:4", "4:3" "4:5", "5:4" "9:16", "16:9" "21:9"

fields.name: imageSize
fields.description: Optional. Specifies the size of generated images. Supported values are `1K`, `2K`, `4K`. If not specified, the model will use default value `1K`.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ImageConfigImageOutputOptions
types.description: The image output format for generated images.

fields.name: mimeType
fields.description: Optional. The image format that the output should be saved as.

fields.name: compressionQuality
fields.description: Optional. The compression quality of the output image.

types.name: query_aiplatform_projects_locations_evaluationRuns_list_evaluationRuns_items_inferenceConfigs_additionalProperties_generationConfig_imageConfig_personGeneration
types.description: Optional. Controls whether the model can generate people.

types.name: query_aiplatform_projects_locations_evaluationRuns_list_evaluationRuns_items_inferenceConfigs_additionalProperties_generationConfig_imageConfig_prominentPeople
types.description: Optional. Controls whether prominent people (celebrities) generation is allowed. If used with personGeneration, personGeneration enum would take precedence. For instance, if ALLOW_NONE is set, all person generation would be blocked. If this field is unspecified, the default behavior is to allow prominent people.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1EvaluationRunInferenceConfigAgentRunConfig
types.description: Configuration for Agent Run.

fields.name: agentEngine
fields.description: Optional. The resource name of the Agent Engine. Format: projects/{project}/locations/{location}/reasoningEngines/{reasoning_engine} For example: projects/123/locations/us-central1/reasoningEngines/456

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1EvaluationRunInferenceConfigSessionInput
types.description: Session input to run an Agent.

fields.name: userId
fields.description: Optional. The user id for the agent session. The ID can be up to 128 characters long.

fields.name: sessionState
fields.description: Optional. Session specific memory which stores key conversation points.

fields.name: parameters
fields.description: Optional. Additional parameters for the session, like app_name, etc. For example, {"app_name": "my-app"}.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1EvaluationRunInferenceConfigAgentRunConfigUserSimulatorConfig
types.description: Used for multi-turn agent scraping. Contains configuration for a user simulator that uses an LLM to generate messages on behalf of the user.

fields.name: modelName
fields.description: The model name to use for multi-turn agent scraping to get next user message, e.g. "gemini-3-flash-preview".

fields.name: maxTurn
fields.description: Maximum number of invocations allowed by the multi-turn agent scraping. This property allows us to stop a run-off conversation, where the agent and the user simulator get into a never ending loop. The initial fixed prompt is also counted as an invocation.

fields.description: Required. The metrics to be calculated in the evaluation run.

fields.name: rubricConfigs
fields.description: Optional. The rubric configs for the evaluation run. They are used to generate rubrics which can be used by rubric-based metrics. Multiple rubric configs can be specified for rubric generation but only one rubric config can be used for a rubric-based metric. If more than one rubric config is provided, the evaluation metric must specify a rubric group key. Note that if a generation spec is specified on both a rubric config and an evaluation metric, the rubrics generated for the metric will be used for evaluation.

fields.name: datasetCustomMetrics
fields.description: Optional. Specifications for custom dataset-level aggregations.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1AutoraterConfig
types.description: The configs for autorater. This is applicable to both EvaluateInstances and EvaluateDataset.

fields.description: Optional. Number of samples for each instance in the dataset. If not specified, the default is 4. Minimum value is 1, maximum value is 32.

fields.name: flipEnabled
fields.description: Optional. Default is true. Whether to flip the candidate and baseline responses. This is only applicable to the pairwise metric. If enabled, also provide PairwiseMetricSpec.candidate_response_field_name and PairwiseMetricSpec.baseline_response_field_name. When rendering PairwiseMetricSpec.metric_prompt_template, the candidate and baseline fields will be flipped for half of the samples to reduce bias.

fields.name: autoraterModel
fields.description: Optional. The fully qualified name of the publisher model or tuned autorater endpoint to use. Publisher model format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/publishers/*/models/*` Tuned model endpoint format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/endpoints/{endpoint}`

types.name: query_aiplatform_projects_locations_evaluationRuns_list_evaluationRuns_items_evaluationConfig_metrics_items_metricConfig_llmBasedMetricSpec_rubricGenerationSpec_rubricContentType
types.description: The type of rubric content to be generated.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1CustomCodeExecutionSpec
types.description: Specificies a metric that is populated by evaluating user-defined Python code.

fields.name: evaluationFunction
fields.description: Required. Python function. Expected user to define the following function, e.g.: def evaluate(instance: dict[str, Any]) -> float: Please include this function signature in the code snippet. Instance is the evaluation instance, any fields populated in the instance are available to the function as instance[field_name]. Example: Example input: ``` instance= EvaluationInstance( response=EvaluationInstance.InstanceData(text="The answer is 4."), reference=EvaluationInstance.InstanceData(text="4") ) ``` Example converted input: ``` { 'response': {'text': 'The answer is 4.'}, 'reference': {'text': '4'} } ``` Example python function: ``` def evaluate(instance: dict[str, Any]) -> float: if instance'response' == instance'reference': return 1.0 return 0.0 ``` CustomCodeExecutionSpec is also supported in Batch Evaluation (EvalDataset RPC) and Tuning Evaluation. Each line in the input jsonl file will be converted to dict[str, Any] and passed to the evaluation function.
