fields.description: Required. The key used to save the generated rubrics. If a generation spec is provided, this key will be used for the name of the generated rubric group. Otherwise, this key will be used to look up the existing rubric group on the evaluation item. Note that if a rubric group key is specified on both a rubric config and an evaluation metric, the key from the metric will be used to select the rubrics for evaluation.

fields.description: Required. Google Cloud Storage URI to output directory. If the uri doesn't end with '/', a '/' will be automatically appended. The directory is created if it doesn't exist.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1EvaluationRunEvaluationConfigPromptTemplate
types.description: Prompt template used for inference.

fields.name: promptTemplate
fields.description: Inline prompt template. Template variables should be in the format "{var_name}". Example: "Translate the following from {source_lang} to {target_lang}: {text}"

fields.name: gcsUri
fields.description: Prompt template stored in Cloud Storage. Format: "gs://my-bucket/file-name.txt".

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1DatasetCustomMetric
types.description: Defines a custom dataset-level aggregation.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: Optional. A display name for this custom summary metric. Used to prefix keys in the output summaryMetrics map. If not provided, a default name like "dataset_custom_metric_1", "dataset_custom_metric_2", etc., will be generated based on the order in the repeated field.

fields.name: aggregationFunction
fields.description: Required. The Python code string containing the aggregation function. Expected function signature: `def aggregate(instances: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> dict[str, float]:` The `instances` argument is a list of dictionaries, where each dictionary represents a single evaluation result item. The structure of each dictionary corresponds to the fields in the `EvaluationResult` message. This includes: - `"request"`: Contains the original input data and model inputs (from `EvaluationResult.EvaluationRequest`). - `"candidate_results"`: Contains the results of any instance-level metrics (from `EvaluationResult.CandidateResults`). Example of a single item in the `instances` list: { "request": { "prompt": {"text": "What is the capital of France?"}, "golden_response": {"text": "Paris"}, "candidate_responses": [{"candidate": "model-v1", "text": "Paris"}] }, "candidate_results": [ {"metric": "exact_match", "score": 1.0}, {"metric": "bleu", "score": 0.9} ] }

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1EvaluationRequest
types.description: A single evaluation request supporting input for both single-turn model generation and multi-turn agent execution traces. Valid input modes: 1. Inference Mode: `prompt` is set (containing text or AgentData context). 2. Offline Eval Mode: `prompt` is unset, and `candidate_responses` contains `agent_data` (the completed execution trace). Validation Rule: Either `prompt` must be set, OR at least one of the `candidate_responses` must contain `agent_data`.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1CachedContent
types.description: A resource used in LLM queries for users to explicitly specify what to cache and how to cache.

fields.name: expireTime
fields.description: Timestamp of when this resource is considered expired. This is *always* provided on output, regardless of what was sent on input.

fields.name: ttl
fields.description: Input only. The TTL for this resource. The expiration time is computed: now + TTL.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Immutable. Identifier. The server-generated resource name of the cached content Format: projects/{project}/locations/{location}/cachedContents/{cached_content}

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: Optional. Immutable. The user-generated meaningful display name of the cached content.

fields.name: model
fields.description: Immutable. The name of the `Model` to use for cached content. Currently, only the published Gemini base models are supported, in form of projects/{PROJECT}/locations/{LOCATION}/publishers/google/models/{MODEL}

fields.name: contents
fields.description: Optional. Input only. Immutable. The content to cache

fields.name: tools
fields.description: Optional. Input only. Immutable. A list of `Tools` the model may use to generate the next response

fields.name: createTime
fields.description: Output only. Creation time of the cache entry.

fields.name: updateTime
fields.description: Output only. When the cache entry was last updated in UTC time.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1Tool
types.description: Tool details that the model may use to generate response. A `Tool` is a piece of code that enables the system to interact with external systems to perform an action, or set of actions, outside of knowledge and scope of the model. A Tool object should contain exactly one type of Tool (e.g FunctionDeclaration, Retrieval or GoogleSearchRetrieval).

fields.name: functionDeclarations
fields.description: Optional. Function tool type. One or more function declarations to be passed to the model along with the current user query. Model may decide to call a subset of these functions by populating FunctionCall in the response. User should provide a FunctionResponse for each function call in the next turn. Based on the function responses, Model will generate the final response back to the user. Maximum 512 function declarations can be provided.

fields.name: codeExecution
fields.description: Tool that executes code generated by the model, and automatically returns the result to the model. See also ExecutableCode and CodeExecutionResult, which are input and output to this tool.

fields.name: urlContext
fields.description: Tool to support URL context.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1FunctionDeclaration
types.description: Structured representation of a function declaration as defined by the [OpenAPI 3.0 specification](https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.0.3). Included in this declaration are the function name, description, parameters and response type. This FunctionDeclaration is a representation of a block of code that can be used as a `Tool` by the model and executed by the client.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Required. The name of the function to call. Must start with a letter or an underscore. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores, dots, colons and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Optional. Description and purpose of the function. Model uses it to decide how and whether to call the function.

fields.name: parametersJsonSchema
fields.description: Optional. Describes the parameters to the function in JSON Schema format. The schema must describe an object where the properties are the parameters to the function. For example: ``` { "type": "object", "properties": { "name": { "type": "string" }, "age": { "type": "integer" } }, "additionalProperties": false, "required": ["name", "age"], "propertyOrdering": ["name", "age"] } ``` This field is mutually exclusive with `parameters`.

fields.name: responseJsonSchema
fields.description: Optional. Describes the output from this function in JSON Schema format. The value specified by the schema is the response value of the function. This field is mutually exclusive with `response`.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1Retrieval
types.description: Defines a retrieval tool that model can call to access external knowledge.

fields.name: disableAttribution
fields.description: Optional. Deprecated. This option is no longer supported.

fields.name: maxResults
fields.description: Optional. Number of search results to return per query. The default value is 10. The maximumm allowed value is 10.

fields.name: dataStoreSpecs
fields.description: Specifications that define the specific DataStores to be searched, along with configurations for those data stores. This is only considered for Engines with multiple data stores. It should only be set if engine is used.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1VertexAISearchDataStoreSpec
types.description: Define data stores within engine to filter on in a search call and configurations for those data stores. For more information, see https://cloud.google.com/generative-ai-app-builder/docs/reference/rpc/google.cloud.discoveryengine.v1#datastorespec

fields.name: dataStore
fields.description: Full resource name of DataStore, such as Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/collections/{collection}/dataStores/{dataStore}`

fields.name: filter
fields.description: Optional. Filter specification to filter documents in the data store specified by data_store field. For more information on filtering, see [Filtering](https://cloud.google.com/generative-ai-app-builder/docs/filter-search-metadata)

types.description: ParallelAiSearch tool type. A tool that uses the Parallel.ai search engine for grounding.

fields.name: apiKey

fields.name: customConfigs
fields.description: Optional. Custom configs for ParallelAiSearch. This field can be used to pass any parameter from the Parallel.ai Search API. See the Parallel.ai documentation for the full list of available parameters and their usage: https://docs.parallel.ai/api-reference/search-beta/search Currently only `source_policy`, `excerpts`, `max_results`, `mode`, `fetch_policy` can be set via this field. For example: { "source_policy": { "include_domains": ["google.com", "wikipedia.org"], "exclude_domains": ["example.com"] }, "fetch_policy": { "max_age_seconds": 3600 } }

Some of them can be explicitly excluded from being automatically included. This can serve two purposes: 1. Using a more restricted / different action space. 2. Improving the definitions / instructions of predefined functions.

For continuous tuning, tuned_model_display_name will by default use the same display name as the pre-tuned model. If a new display name is provided, the tuning job will create a new model instead of a new version.

fields.description: Optional. The description of the TuningJob.

fields.name: updateTime

fields.description: Optional. The labels with user-defined metadata to organize TuningJob and generated resources such as Model and Endpoint. Label keys and values can be no longer than 64 characters (Unicode codepoints), can only contain lowercase letters, numeric characters, underscores and dashes. International characters are allowed. See https://goo.gl/xmQnxf for more information and examples of labels.

See https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/service-agents#vertex-ai-secure-fine-tuning-service-agent Users starting the pipeline must have the `iam.serviceAccounts.actAs` permission on this service account.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1Index
types.description: A representation of a collection of database items organized in a way that allows for approximate nearest neighbor (a.k.a ANN) algorithms search.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Output only. The resource name of the Index.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: Required. The display name of the Index. The name can be up to 128 characters long and can consist of any UTF-8 characters.

fields.name: description
fields.description: The description of the Index.

fields.name: metadataSchemaUri
fields.description: Immutable. Points to a YAML file stored on Google Cloud Storage describing additional information about the Index, that is specific to it. Unset if the Index does not have any additional information. The schema is defined as an OpenAPI 3.0.2 [Schema Object](https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/main/versions/3.0.2.md#schemaObject). Note: The URI given on output will be immutable and probably different, including the URI scheme, than the one given on input. The output URI will point to a location where the user only has a read access.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: An additional information about the Index; the schema of the metadata can be found in metadata_schema.

fields.name: deployedIndexes
fields.description: Output only. The pointers to DeployedIndexes created from this Index. An Index can be only deleted if all its DeployedIndexes had been undeployed first.

fields.name: etag
fields.description: Used to perform consistent read-modify-write updates. If not set, a blind "overwrite" update happens.

fields.name: labels
fields.description: The labels with user-defined metadata to organize your Indexes. Label keys and values can be no longer than 64 characters (Unicode codepoints), can only contain lowercase letters, numeric characters, underscores and dashes. International characters are allowed. See https://goo.gl/xmQnxf for more information and examples of labels.

fields.name: createTime
fields.description: Output only. Timestamp when this Index was created.

fields.name: updateTime
fields.description: Output only. Timestamp when this Index was most recently updated. This also includes any update to the contents of the Index. Note that Operations working on this Index may have their Operations.metadata.generic_metadata.update_time a little after the value of this timestamp, yet that does not mean their results are not already reflected in the Index. Result of any successfully completed Operation on the Index is reflected in it.

fields.name: satisfiesPzs
fields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

fields.name: satisfiesPzi
fields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

types.description: Points to a DeployedIndex.

fields.name: indexEndpoint
fields.description: Immutable. A resource name of the IndexEndpoint.

fields.name: deployedIndexId
fields.description: Immutable. The ID of the DeployedIndex in the above IndexEndpoint.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: Output only. The display name of the DeployedIndex.

types.description: Stats of the Index.

fields.name: sparseVectorsCount
fields.description: Output only. The number of sparse vectors in the Index.

fields.name: shardsCount
fields.description: Output only. The number of shards in the Index.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1IndexEndpoint
types.description: Indexes are deployed into it. An IndexEndpoint can have multiple DeployedIndexes.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Output only. The resource name of the IndexEndpoint.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: Required. The display name of the IndexEndpoint. The name can be up to 128 characters long and can consist of any UTF-8 characters.

fields.name: description
fields.description: The description of the IndexEndpoint.

fields.name: deployedIndexes
fields.description: Output only. The indexes deployed in this endpoint.

fields.name: etag
fields.description: Used to perform consistent read-modify-write updates. If not set, a blind "overwrite" update happens.

fields.name: labels
fields.description: The labels with user-defined metadata to organize your IndexEndpoints. Label keys and values can be no longer than 64 characters (Unicode codepoints), can only contain lowercase letters, numeric characters, underscores and dashes. International characters are allowed. See https://goo.gl/xmQnxf for more information and examples of labels.

fields.name: createTime
fields.description: Output only. Timestamp when this IndexEndpoint was created.

fields.name: updateTime
fields.description: Output only. Timestamp when this IndexEndpoint was last updated. This timestamp is not updated when the endpoint's DeployedIndexes are updated, e.g. due to updates of the original Indexes they are the deployments of.

fields.name: network
fields.description: Optional. The full name of the Google Compute Engine [network](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/networks-and-firewalls#networks) to which the IndexEndpoint should be peered. Private services access must already be configured for the network. If left unspecified, the Endpoint is not peered with any network. network and private_service_connect_config are mutually exclusive. [Format](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/reference/rest/v1/networks/insert): `projects/{project}/global/networks/{network}`. Where {project} is a project number, as in '12345', and {network} is network name.

fields.name: enablePrivateServiceConnect
fields.description: Optional. Deprecated: If true, expose the IndexEndpoint via private service connect. Only one of the fields, network or enable_private_service_connect, can be set.

fields.name: publicEndpointEnabled
fields.description: Optional. If true, the deployed index will be accessible through public endpoint.

fields.name: publicEndpointDomainName
fields.description: Output only. If public_endpoint_enabled is true, this field will be populated with the domain name to use for this index endpoint.

fields.name: satisfiesPzs
fields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

fields.name: satisfiesPzi
fields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1DeployedIndex
types.description: A deployment of an Index. IndexEndpoints contain one or more DeployedIndexes.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Required. The user specified ID of the DeployedIndex. The ID can be up to 128 characters long and must start with a letter and only contain letters, numbers, and underscores. The ID must be unique within the project it is created in.

fields.name: index
fields.description: Required. The name of the Index this is the deployment of. We may refer to this Index as the DeployedIndex's "original" Index.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: The display name of the DeployedIndex. If not provided upon creation, the Index's display_name is used.

fields.name: createTime
fields.description: Output only. Timestamp when the DeployedIndex was created.

fields.name: indexSyncTime
fields.description: Output only. The DeployedIndex may depend on various data on its original Index. Additionally when certain changes to the original Index are being done (e.g. when what the Index contains is being changed) the DeployedIndex may be asynchronously updated in the background to reflect these changes. If this timestamp's value is at least the Index.update_time of the original Index, it means that this DeployedIndex and the original Index are in sync. If this timestamp is older, then to see which updates this DeployedIndex already contains (and which it does not), one must list the operations that are running on the original Index. Only the successfully completed Operations with update_time equal or before this sync time are contained in this DeployedIndex.

fields.name: enableAccessLogging
fields.description: Optional. If true, private endpoint's access logs are sent to Cloud Logging. These logs are like standard server access logs, containing information like timestamp and latency for each MatchRequest. Note that logs may incur a cost, especially if the deployed index receives a high queries per second rate (QPS). Estimate your costs before enabling this option.

fields.name: enableDatapointUpsertLogging
fields.description: Optional. If true, logs to Cloud Logging errors relating to datapoint upserts. Under normal operation conditions, these log entries should be very rare. However, if incompatible datapoint updates are being uploaded to an index, a high volume of log entries may be generated in a short period of time. Note that logs may incur a cost, especially if the deployed index receives a high volume of datapoint upserts. Estimate your costs before enabling this option.

fields.name: reservedIpRanges
fields.description: Optional. A list of reserved ip ranges under the VPC network that can be used for this DeployedIndex. If set, we will deploy the index within the provided ip ranges. Otherwise, the index might be deployed to any ip ranges under the provided VPC network. The value should be the name of the address (https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/reference/rest/v1/addresses) Example: ['vertex-ai-ip-range']. For more information about subnets and network IP ranges, please see https://cloud.google.com/vpc/docs/subnets#manually_created_subnet_ip_ranges.

fields.name: deploymentGroup
fields.description: Optional. The deployment group can be no longer than 64 characters (eg: 'test', 'prod'). If not set, we will use the 'default' deployment group. Creating `deployment_groups` with `reserved_ip_ranges` is a recommended practice when the peered network has multiple peering ranges. This creates your deployments from predictable IP spaces for easier traffic administration. Also, one deployment_group (except 'default') can only be used with the same reserved_ip_ranges which means if the deployment_group has been used with reserved_ip_ranges: [a, b, c], using it with [a, b] or [d, e] is disallowed. Note: we only support up to 5 deployment groups(not including 'default').

fields.name: pscAutomationConfigs
fields.description: Optional. If set for PSC deployed index, PSC connection will be automatically created after deployment is done and the endpoint information is populated in private_endpoints.psc_automated_endpoints.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1IndexPrivateEndpoints
types.description: IndexPrivateEndpoints proto is used to provide paths for users to send requests via private endpoints (e.g. private service access, private service connect). To send request via private service access, use match_grpc_address. To send request via private service connect, use service_attachment.

fields.name: matchGrpcAddress
fields.description: Output only. The ip address used to send match gRPC requests.

fields.name: serviceAttachment
fields.description: Output only. The name of the service attachment resource. Populated if private service connect is enabled.

fields.name: pscAutomatedEndpoints
fields.description: Output only. PscAutomatedEndpoints is populated if private service connect is enabled if PscAutomatedConfig is set.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1PscAutomatedEndpoints
types.description: PscAutomatedEndpoints defines the output of the forwarding rule automatically created by each PscAutomationConfig.

fields.name: projectId
fields.description: Corresponding project_id in pscAutomationConfigs

fields.name: network
fields.description: Corresponding network in pscAutomationConfigs.

fields.name: matchAddress
fields.description: Ip Address created by the automated forwarding rule.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1DeployedIndexAuthConfig
types.description: Used to set up the auth on the DeployedIndex's private endpoint.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1DeployedIndexAuthConfigAuthProvider
types.description: Configuration for an authentication provider, including support for [JSON Web Token (JWT)](https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-ietf-oauth-json-web-token-32).

fields.name: audiences
fields.description: The list of JWT [audiences](https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-ietf-oauth-json-web-token-32#section-4.1.3). that are allowed to access. A JWT containing any of these audiences will be accepted.

fields.name: allowedIssuers
fields.description: A list of allowed JWT issuers. Each entry must be a valid Google service account, in the following format: `service-account-name@project-id.iam.gserviceaccount.com`

types.name: query_aiplatform_projects_locations_indexEndpoints_list_indexEndpoints_items_deployedIndexes_items_deploymentTier
types.description: Optional. The deployment tier that the index is deployed to. DEPLOYMENT_TIER_UNSPECIFIED will use a system-chosen default tier.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1CustomJob
types.description: Represents a job that runs custom workloads such as a Docker container or a Python package. A CustomJob can have multiple worker pools and each worker pool can have its own machine and input spec. A CustomJob will be cleaned up once the job enters terminal state (failed or succeeded).

fields.name: name
fields.description: Output only. Resource name of a CustomJob.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: Required. The display name of the CustomJob. The name can be up to 128 characters long and can consist of any UTF-8 characters.

fields.name: createTime
fields.description: Output only. Time when the CustomJob was created.

fields.name: startTime
fields.description: Output only. Time when the CustomJob for the first time entered the `JOB_STATE_RUNNING` state.

fields.name: endTime
fields.description: Output only. Time when the CustomJob entered any of the following states: `JOB_STATE_SUCCEEDED`, `JOB_STATE_FAILED`, `JOB_STATE_CANCELLED`.

fields.name: updateTime
fields.description: Output only. Time when the CustomJob was most recently updated.

fields.name: labels
fields.description: The labels with user-defined metadata to organize CustomJobs. Label keys and values can be no longer than 64 characters (Unicode codepoints), can only contain lowercase letters, numeric characters, underscores and dashes. International characters are allowed. See https://goo.gl/xmQnxf for more information and examples of labels.

fields.name: webAccessUris
fields.description: Output only. URIs for accessing [interactive shells](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/training/monitor-debug-interactive-shell) (one URI for each training node). Only available if job_spec.enable_web_access is `true`. The keys are names of each node in the training job; for example, `workerpool0-0` for the primary node, `workerpool1-0` for the first node in the second worker pool, and `workerpool1-1` for the second node in the second worker pool. The values are the URIs for each node's interactive shell.

fields.name: satisfiesPzs
fields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

fields.name: satisfiesPzi
fields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1CustomJobSpec
types.description: Represents the spec of a CustomJob.

fields.name: persistentResourceId
fields.description: Optional. The ID of the PersistentResource in the same Project and Location which to run If this is specified, the job will be run on existing machines held by the PersistentResource instead of on-demand short-live machines. The network and CMEK configs on the job should be consistent with those on the PersistentResource, otherwise, the job will be rejected.

fields.name: workerPoolSpecs
fields.description: Required. The spec of the worker pools including machine type and Docker image. All worker pools except the first one are optional and can be skipped by providing an empty value.

fields.name: serviceAccount
fields.description: Specifies the service account for workload run-as account. Users submitting jobs must have act-as permission on this run-as account. If unspecified, the [Vertex AI Custom Code Service Agent](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/general/access-control#service-agents) for the CustomJob's project is used.

fields.name: network
fields.description: Optional. The full name of the Compute Engine [network](/compute/docs/networks-and-firewalls#networks) to which the Job should be peered. For example, `projects/12345/global/networks/myVPC`. [Format](/compute/docs/reference/rest/v1/networks/insert) is of the form `projects/{project}/global/networks/{network}`. Where {project} is a project number, as in `12345`, and {network} is a network name. To specify this field, you must have already [configured VPC Network Peering for Vertex AI](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/general/vpc-peering). If this field is left unspecified, the job is not peered with any network.

fields.name: reservedIpRanges
fields.description: Optional. A list of names for the reserved ip ranges under the VPC network that can be used for this job. If set, we will deploy the job within the provided ip ranges. Otherwise, the job will be deployed to any ip ranges under the provided VPC network. Example: ['vertex-ai-ip-range'].

fields.name: protectedArtifactLocationId
fields.description: The ID of the location to store protected artifacts. e.g. us-central1. Populate only when the location is different than CustomJob location. List of supported locations: https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/general/locations

fields.name: tensorboard
fields.description: Optional. The name of a Vertex AI Tensorboard resource to which this CustomJob will upload Tensorboard logs. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/tensorboards/{tensorboard}`

fields.name: enableWebAccess
fields.description: Optional. Whether you want Vertex AI to enable [interactive shell access](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/training/monitor-debug-interactive-shell) to training containers. If set to `true`, you can access interactive shells at the URIs given by CustomJob.web_access_uris or Trial.web_access_uris (within HyperparameterTuningJob.trials).

fields.name: enableDashboardAccess
fields.description: Optional. Whether you want Vertex AI to enable access to the customized dashboard in training chief container. If set to `true`, you can access the dashboard at the URIs given by CustomJob.web_access_uris or Trial.web_access_uris (within HyperparameterTuningJob.trials).

fields.name: experiment
fields.description: Optional. The Experiment associated with this job. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/metadataStores/{metadataStores}/contexts/{experiment-name}`

fields.name: experimentRun
fields.description: Optional. The Experiment Run associated with this job. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/metadataStores/{metadataStores}/contexts/{experiment-name}-{experiment-run-name}`

fields.name: models
fields.description: Optional. The name of the Model resources for which to generate a mapping to artifact URIs. Applicable only to some of the Google-provided custom jobs. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/models/{model}` In order to retrieve a specific version of the model, also provide the version ID or version alias. Example: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/models/{model}@2` or `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/models/{model}@golden` If no version ID or alias is specified, the "default" version will be returned. The "default" version alias is created for the first version of the model, and can be moved to other versions later on. There will be exactly one default version.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1WorkerPoolSpec
types.description: Represents the spec of a worker pool in a job.

fields.name: replicaCount
fields.description: Optional. The number of worker replicas to use for this worker pool.

fields.name: nfsMounts
fields.description: Optional. List of NFS mount spec.

fields.name: lustreMounts
fields.description: Optional. List of Lustre mounts.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ContainerSpec
types.description: The spec of a Container.

fields.name: imageUri
fields.description: Required. The URI of a container image in the Container Registry that is to be run on each worker replica.

fields.name: command
fields.description: The command to be invoked when the container is started. It overrides the entrypoint instruction in Dockerfile when provided.

fields.name: args
fields.description: The arguments to be passed when starting the container.

fields.name: env
fields.description: Environment variables to be passed to the container. Maximum limit is 100.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1PythonPackageSpec
types.description: The spec of a Python packaged code.

fields.name: executorImageUri
fields.description: Required. The URI of a container image in Artifact Registry that will run the provided Python package. Vertex AI provides a wide range of executor images with pre-installed packages to meet users' various use cases. See the list of [pre-built containers for training](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/training/pre-built-containers). You must use an image from this list.

fields.name: packageUris
fields.description: Required. The Google Cloud Storage location of the Python package files which are the training program and its dependent packages. The maximum number of package URIs is 100.

fields.name: pythonModule
fields.description: Required. The Python module name to run after installing the packages.

fields.name: args
fields.description: Command line arguments to be passed to the Python task.

fields.name: env
fields.description: Environment variables to be passed to the python module. Maximum limit is 100.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1NfsMount
types.description: Represents a mount configuration for Network File System (NFS) to mount.

fields.name: server
fields.description: Required. IP address of the NFS server.

fields.name: path
fields.description: Required. Source path exported from NFS server. Has to start with '/', and combined with the ip address, it indicates the source mount path in the form of `server:path`

fields.name: mountPoint
fields.description: Required. Destination mount path. The NFS will be mounted for the user under /mnt/nfs/

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1Scheduling
types.description: All parameters related to queuing and scheduling of custom jobs.

fields.name: timeout
fields.description: Optional. The maximum job running time. The default is 7 days.

fields.name: restartJobOnWorkerRestart
fields.description: Optional. Restarts the entire CustomJob if a worker gets restarted. This feature can be used by distributed training jobs that are not resilient to workers leaving and joining a job.

fields.name: disableRetries
fields.description: Optional. Indicates if the job should retry for internal errors after the job starts running. If true, overrides `Scheduling.restart_job_on_worker_restart` to false.

fields.name: maxWaitDuration
fields.description: Optional. This is the maximum duration that a job will wait for the requested resources to be provisioned if the scheduling strategy is set to [Strategy.DWS_FLEX_START]. If set to 0, the job will wait indefinitely. The default is 24 hours.

types.description: Optional. This determines which type of scheduling strategy to use.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1DataLabelingJob
types.description: DataLabelingJob is used to trigger a human labeling job on unlabeled data from the following Dataset:

fields.name: name
fields.description: Output only. Resource name of the DataLabelingJob.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: Required. The user-defined name of the DataLabelingJob. The name can be up to 128 characters long and can consist of any UTF-8 characters. Display name of a DataLabelingJob.

fields.name: datasets
fields.description: Required. Dataset resource names. Right now we only support labeling from a single Dataset. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/datasets/{dataset}`

fields.name: annotationLabels
fields.description: Labels to assign to annotations generated by this DataLabelingJob. Label keys and values can be no longer than 64 characters (Unicode codepoints), can only contain lowercase letters, numeric characters, underscores and dashes. International characters are allowed. See https://goo.gl/xmQnxf for more information and examples of labels. System reserved label keys are prefixed with "aiplatform.googleapis.com/" and are immutable.

fields.name: labelerCount
fields.description: Required. Number of labelers to work on each DataItem.

fields.name: instructionUri
fields.description: Required. The Google Cloud Storage location of the instruction pdf. This pdf is shared with labelers, and provides detailed description on how to label DataItems in Datasets.

fields.name: inputsSchemaUri
fields.description: Required. Points to a YAML file stored on Google Cloud Storage describing the config for a specific type of DataLabelingJob. The schema files that can be used here are found in the https://storage.googleapis.com/google-cloud-aiplatform bucket in the /schema/datalabelingjob/inputs/ folder.

fields.name: inputs
fields.description: Required. Input config parameters for the DataLabelingJob.

fields.name: labelingProgress
fields.description: Output only. Current labeling job progress percentage scaled in interval [0, 100], indicating the percentage of DataItems that has been finished.

fields.name: createTime
fields.description: Output only. Timestamp when this DataLabelingJob was created.

fields.name: updateTime
fields.description: Output only. Timestamp when this DataLabelingJob was updated most recently.

fields.name: labels
fields.description: The labels with user-defined metadata to organize your DataLabelingJobs. Label keys and values can be no longer than 64 characters (Unicode codepoints), can only contain lowercase letters, numeric characters, underscores and dashes. International characters are allowed. See https://goo.gl/xmQnxf for more information and examples of labels. System reserved label keys are prefixed with "aiplatform.googleapis.com/" and are immutable. Following system labels exist for each DataLabelingJob: * "aiplatform.googleapis.com/schema": output only, its value is the inputs_schema's title.

fields.name: specialistPools
fields.description: The SpecialistPools' resource names associated with this job.

types.name: query_aiplatform_projects_locations_dataLabelingJobs_list_dataLabelingJobs_items_state
types.description: Output only. The detailed state of the job.

types.name: GoogleTypeMoney
types.description: Represents an amount of money with its currency type.

fields.name: currencyCode
fields.description: The three-letter currency code defined in ISO 4217.

fields.name: units
fields.description: The whole units of the amount. For example if `currencyCode` is `"USD"`, then 1 unit is one US dollar.

fields.name: nanos
fields.description: Number of nano (10^-9) units of the amount. The value must be between -999,999,999 and +999,999,999 inclusive. If `units` is positive, `nanos` must be positive or zero. If `units` is zero, `nanos` can be positive, zero, or negative. If `units` is negative, `nanos` must be negative or zero. For example $-1.75 is represented as `units`=-1 and `nanos`=-750,000,000.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ActiveLearningConfig
types.description: Parameters that configure the active learning pipeline. Active learning will label the data incrementally by several iterations. For every iteration, it will select a batch of data based on the sampling strategy.

fields.name: maxDataItemCount
fields.description: Max number of human labeled DataItems.

fields.name: maxDataItemPercentage
fields.description: Max percent of total DataItems for human labeling.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1SampleConfig
types.description: Active learning data sampling config. For every active learning labeling iteration, it will select a batch of data based on the sampling strategy.

fields.name: initialBatchSamplePercentage
fields.description: The percentage of data needed to be labeled in the first batch.

fields.name: followingBatchSamplePercentage
fields.description: The percentage of data needed to be labeled in each following batch (except the first batch).

types.name: query_aiplatform_projects_locations_dataLabelingJobs_list_dataLabelingJobs_items_activeLearningConfig_sampleConfig_sampleStrategy
types.description: Field to choose sampling strategy. Sampling strategy will decide which data should be selected for human labeling in every batch.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1TrainingConfig
types.description: CMLE training config. For every active learning labeling iteration, system will train a machine learning model on CMLE. The trained model will be used by data sampling algorithm to select DataItems.

fields.name: timeoutTrainingMilliHours
fields.description: The timeout hours for the CMLE training job, expressed in milli hours i.e. 1,000 value in this field means 1 hour.

fields.description: Required. The display name of the HyperparameterTuningJob. The name can be up to 128 characters long and can consist of any UTF-8 characters.

fields.name: maxTrialCount

fields.name: parallelTrialCount

fields.name: maxFailedTrialCount
fields.description: The number of failed Trials that need to be seen before failing the HyperparameterTuningJob. If set to 0, Vertex AI decides how many Trials must fail before the whole job fails.

fields.name: trials

fields.name: createTime

fields.name: endTime

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1StudySpec
types.description: Represents specification of a Study.

fields.name: metrics
fields.description: Required. Metric specs for the Study.

fields.name: parameters
fields.description: Required. The set of parameters to tune.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1StudySpecDecayCurveAutomatedStoppingSpec
types.description: The decay curve automated stopping rule builds a Gaussian Process Regressor to predict the final objective value of a Trial based on the already completed Trials and the intermediate measurements of the current Trial. Early stopping is requested for the current Trial if there is very low probability to exceed the optimal value found so far.

fields.name: useElapsedDuration
fields.description: True if Measurement.elapsed_duration is used as the x-axis of each Trials Decay Curve. Otherwise, Measurement.step_count will be used as the x-axis.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1StudySpecMedianAutomatedStoppingSpec
types.description: The median automated stopping rule stops a pending Trial if the Trial's best objective_value is strictly below the median 'performance' of all completed Trials reported up to the Trial's last measurement. Currently, 'performance' refers to the running average of the objective values reported by the Trial in each measurement.

fields.name: useElapsedDuration
fields.description: True if median automated stopping rule applies on Measurement.elapsed_duration. It means that elapsed_duration field of latest measurement of current Trial is used to compute median objective value for each completed Trials.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1StudySpecConvexAutomatedStoppingSpec
types.description: Configuration for ConvexAutomatedStoppingSpec. When there are enough completed trials (configured by min_measurement_count), for pending trials with enough measurements and steps, the policy first computes an overestimate of the objective value at max_num_steps according to the slope of the incomplete objective value curve. No prediction can be made if the curve is completely flat. If the overestimation is worse than the best objective value of the completed trials, this pending trial will be early-stopped, but a last measurement will be added to the pending trial with max_num_steps and predicted objective value from the autoregression model.

fields.name: maxStepCount
fields.description: Steps used in predicting the final objective for early stopped trials. In general, it's set to be the same as the defined steps in training / tuning. If not defined, it will learn it from the completed trials. When use_steps is false, this field is set to the maximum elapsed seconds.

fields.name: minStepCount
fields.description: Minimum number of steps for a trial to complete. Trials which do not have a measurement with step_count > min_step_count won't be considered for early stopping. It's ok to set it to 0, and a trial can be early stopped at any stage. By default, min_step_count is set to be one-tenth of the max_step_count. When use_elapsed_duration is true, this field is set to the minimum elapsed seconds.

fields.name: minMeasurementCount
fields.description: The minimal number of measurements in a Trial. Early-stopping checks will not trigger if less than min_measurement_count+1 completed trials or pending trials with less than min_measurement_count measurements. If not defined, the default value is 5.

fields.name: learningRateParameterName
fields.description: The hyper-parameter name used in the tuning job that stands for learning rate. Leave it blank if learning rate is not in a parameter in tuning. The learning_rate is used to estimate the objective value of the ongoing trial.

fields.name: useElapsedDuration
fields.description: This bool determines whether or not the rule is applied based on elapsed_secs or steps. If use_elapsed_duration==false, the early stopping decision is made according to the predicted objective values according to the target steps. If use_elapsed_duration==true, elapsed_secs is used instead of steps. Also, in this case, the parameters max_num_steps and min_num_steps are overloaded to contain max_elapsed_seconds and min_elapsed_seconds.

fields.name: updateAllStoppedTrials
fields.description: ConvexAutomatedStoppingSpec by default only updates the trials that needs to be early stopped using a newly trained auto-regressive model. When this flag is set to True, all stopped trials from the beginning are potentially updated in terms of their `final_measurement`. Also, note that the training logic of autoregressive models is different in this case. Enabling this option has shown better results and this may be the default option in the future.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1StudySpecMetricSpec
types.description: Represents a metric to optimize.

fields.name: metricId
fields.description: Required. The ID of the metric. Must not contain whitespaces and must be unique amongst all MetricSpecs.

types.name: query_aiplatform_projects_locations_hyperparameterTuningJobs_list_hyperparameterTuningJobs_items_studySpec_metrics_items_goal
types.description: Required. The optimization goal of the metric.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1StudySpecMetricSpecSafetyMetricConfig
types.description: Used in safe optimization to specify threshold levels and risk tolerance.

fields.name: safetyThreshold
fields.description: Safety threshold (boundary value between safe and unsafe). NOTE that if you leave SafetyMetricConfig unset, a default value of 0 will be used.

fields.name: desiredMinSafeTrialsFraction
fields.description: Desired minimum fraction of safe trials (over total number of trials) that should be targeted by the algorithm at any time during the study (best effort). This should be between 0.0 and 1.0 and a value of 0.0 means that there is no minimum and an algorithm proceeds without targeting any specific fraction. A value of 1.0 means that the algorithm attempts to only Suggest safe Trials.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1StudySpecParameterSpec
types.description: Represents a single parameter to optimize.

fields.name: parameterId
fields.description: Required. The ID of the parameter. Must not contain whitespaces and must be unique amongst all ParameterSpecs.

fields.name: conditionalParameterSpecs
fields.description: A conditional parameter node is active if the parameter's value matches the conditional node's parent_value_condition. If two items in conditional_parameter_specs have the same name, they must have disjoint parent_value_condition.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1StudySpecParameterSpecDoubleValueSpec
types.description: Value specification for a parameter in `DOUBLE` type.

fields.name: minValue
fields.description: Required. Inclusive minimum value of the parameter.

fields.name: maxValue
fields.description: Required. Inclusive maximum value of the parameter.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A default value for a `DOUBLE` parameter that is assumed to be a relatively good starting point. Unset value signals that there is no offered starting point. Currently only supported by the Vertex AI Vizier service. Not supported by HyperparameterTuningJob or TrainingPipeline.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1StudySpecParameterSpecIntegerValueSpec
types.description: Value specification for a parameter in `INTEGER` type.

fields.name: minValue
fields.description: Required. Inclusive minimum value of the parameter.

fields.name: maxValue
fields.description: Required. Inclusive maximum value of the parameter.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A default value for an `INTEGER` parameter that is assumed to be a relatively good starting point. Unset value signals that there is no offered starting point. Currently only supported by the Vertex AI Vizier service. Not supported by HyperparameterTuningJob or TrainingPipeline.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1StudySpecParameterSpecCategoricalValueSpec
types.description: Value specification for a parameter in `CATEGORICAL` type.

fields.name: values
fields.description: Required. The list of possible categories.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A default value for a `CATEGORICAL` parameter that is assumed to be a relatively good starting point. Unset value signals that there is no offered starting point. Currently only supported by the Vertex AI Vizier service. Not supported by HyperparameterTuningJob or TrainingPipeline.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1StudySpecParameterSpecDiscreteValueSpec
types.description: Value specification for a parameter in `DISCRETE` type.

fields.name: values
fields.description: Required. A list of possible values. The list should be in increasing order and at least 1e-10 apart. For instance, this parameter might have possible settings of 1.5, 2.5, and 4.0. This list should not contain more than 1,000 values.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A default value for a `DISCRETE` parameter that is assumed to be a relatively good starting point. Unset value signals that there is no offered starting point. It automatically rounds to the nearest feasible discrete point. Currently only supported by the Vertex AI Vizier service. Not supported by HyperparameterTuningJob or TrainingPipeline.

types.name: query_aiplatform_projects_locations_hyperparameterTuningJobs_list_hyperparameterTuningJobs_items_studySpec_parameters_items_scaleType
types.description: How the parameter should be scaled. Leave unset for `CATEGORICAL` parameters.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1StudySpecParameterSpecConditionalParameterSpec
types.description: Represents a parameter spec with condition from its parent parameter.

fields.name: parameterSpec
fields.description: Represents a single parameter to optimize.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1StudySpecParameterSpecConditionalParameterSpecDiscreteValueCondition
types.description: Represents the spec to match discrete values from parent parameter.

fields.name: values
fields.description: Required. Matches values of the parent parameter of 'DISCRETE' type. All values must exist in `discrete_value_spec` of parent parameter. The Epsilon of the value matching is 1e-10.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1StudySpecParameterSpecConditionalParameterSpecIntValueCondition
types.description: Represents the spec to match integer values from parent parameter.

fields.name: values
fields.description: Required. Matches values of the parent parameter of 'INTEGER' type. All values must lie in `integer_value_spec` of parent parameter.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1StudySpecParameterSpecConditionalParameterSpecCategoricalValueCondition
types.description: Represents the spec to match categorical values from parent parameter.

fields.name: values
fields.description: Required. Matches values of the parent parameter of 'CATEGORICAL' type. All values must exist in `categorical_value_spec` of parent parameter.

types.name: query_aiplatform_projects_locations_hyperparameterTuningJobs_list_hyperparameterTuningJobs_items_studySpec_algorithm
types.description: The search algorithm specified for the Study.

types.name: query_aiplatform_projects_locations_hyperparameterTuningJobs_list_hyperparameterTuningJobs_items_studySpec_observationNoise
types.description: The observation noise level of the study. Currently only supported by the Vertex AI Vizier service. Not supported by HyperparameterTuningJob or TrainingPipeline.

types.name: query_aiplatform_projects_locations_hyperparameterTuningJobs_list_hyperparameterTuningJobs_items_studySpec_measurementSelectionType
types.description: Describe which measurement selection type will be used

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1StudySpecStudyStoppingConfig
types.description: The configuration (stopping conditions) for automated stopping of a Study. Conditions include trial budgets, time budgets, and convergence detection.

fields.name: shouldStopAsap
fields.description: If true, a Study enters STOPPING_ASAP whenever it would normally enters STOPPING state. The bottom line is: set to true if you want to interrupt on-going evaluations of Trials as soon as the study stopping condition is met. (Please see Study.State documentation for the source of truth).

fields.name: minNumTrials
fields.description: If there are fewer than this many COMPLETED trials, do not stop the study.

fields.name: maxNumTrials
fields.description: If there are more than this many trials, stop the study.

fields.name: maxNumTrialsNoProgress
fields.description: If the objective value has not improved for this many consecutive trials, stop the study. WARNING: Effective only for single-objective studies.

fields.name: maxDurationNoProgress
fields.description: If the objective value has not improved for this much time, stop the study. WARNING: Effective only for single-objective studies.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1StudyTimeConstraint
types.description: Time-based Constraint for Study

fields.name: maxDuration
fields.description: Counts the wallclock time passed since the creation of this Study.

fields.name: endTime
fields.description: Compares the wallclock time to this time. Must use UTC timezone.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1Trial
types.description: A message representing a Trial. A Trial contains a unique set of Parameters that has been or will be evaluated, along with the objective metrics got by running the Trial.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Output only. Resource name of the Trial assigned by the service.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Output only. The identifier of the Trial assigned by the service.

fields.name: parameters
fields.description: Output only. The parameters of the Trial.

fields.name: measurements
fields.description: Output only. A list of measurements that are strictly lexicographically ordered by their induced tuples (steps, elapsed_duration). These are used for early stopping computations.

fields.name: startTime
fields.description: Output only. Time when the Trial was started.

fields.name: endTime
fields.description: Output only. Time when the Trial's status changed to `SUCCEEDED` or `INFEASIBLE`.

fields.name: clientId
fields.description: Output only. The identifier of the client that originally requested this Trial. Each client is identified by a unique client_id. When a client asks for a suggestion, Vertex AI Vizier will assign it a Trial. The client should evaluate the Trial, complete it, and report back to Vertex AI Vizier. If suggestion is asked again by same client_id before the Trial is completed, the same Trial will be returned. Multiple clients with different client_ids can ask for suggestions simultaneously, each of them will get their own Trial.

fields.name: infeasibleReason
fields.description: Output only. A human readable string describing why the Trial is infeasible. This is set only if Trial state is `INFEASIBLE`.

fields.name: customJob
fields.description: Output only. The CustomJob name linked to the Trial. It's set for a HyperparameterTuningJob's Trial.

fields.name: webAccessUris
fields.description: Output only. URIs for accessing [interactive shells](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/training/monitor-debug-interactive-shell) (one URI for each training node). Only available if this trial is part of a HyperparameterTuningJob and the job's trial_job_spec.enable_web_access field is `true`. The keys are names of each node used for the trial; for example, `workerpool0-0` for the primary node, `workerpool1-0` for the first node in the second worker pool, and `workerpool1-1` for the second node in the second worker pool. The values are the URIs for each node's interactive shell.

types.name: query_aiplatform_projects_locations_hyperparameterTuningJobs_list_hyperparameterTuningJobs_items_trials_items_state
types.description: Output only. The detailed state of the Trial.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1TrialParameter
types.description: A message representing a parameter to be tuned.

fields.name: parameterId
fields.description: Output only. The ID of the parameter. The parameter should be defined in StudySpec's Parameters.

fields.name: value
fields.description: Output only. The value of the parameter. `number_value` will be set if a parameter defined in StudySpec is in type 'INTEGER', 'DOUBLE' or 'DISCRETE'. `string_value` will be set if a parameter defined in StudySpec is in type 'CATEGORICAL'.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1Measurement
types.description: A message representing a Measurement of a Trial. A Measurement contains the Metrics got by executing a Trial using suggested hyperparameter values.

fields.name: elapsedDuration
fields.description: Output only. Time that the Trial has been running at the point of this Measurement.

fields.name: stepCount
fields.description: Output only. The number of steps the machine learning model has been trained for. Must be non-negative.

fields.name: metrics
fields.description: Output only. A list of metrics got by evaluating the objective functions using suggested Parameter values.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1MeasurementMetric
types.description: A message representing a metric in the measurement.

fields.name: metricId
fields.description: Output only. The ID of the Metric. The Metric should be defined in StudySpec's Metrics.

fields.name: value
fields.description: Output only. The value for this metric.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1BatchPredictionJob
types.description: A job that uses a Model to produce predictions on multiple input instances. If predictions for significant portion of the instances fail, the job may finish without attempting predictions for all remaining instances.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Output only. Resource name of the BatchPredictionJob.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: Required. The user-defined name of this BatchPredictionJob.

fields.name: model
fields.description: The name of the Model resource that produces the predictions via this job, must share the same ancestor Location. Starting this job has no impact on any existing deployments of the Model and their resources. Exactly one of model, unmanaged_container_model, or endpoint must be set. The model resource name may contain version id or version alias to specify the version. Example: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/models/{model}@2` or `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/models/{model}@golden` if no version is specified, the default version will be deployed. The model resource could also be a publisher model. Example: `publishers/{publisher}/models/{model}` or `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/publishers/{publisher}/models/{model}`

fields.name: modelVersionId
fields.description: Output only. The version ID of the Model that produces the predictions via this job.

fields.name: modelParameters
fields.description: The parameters that govern the predictions. The schema of the parameters may be specified via the Model's PredictSchemata's parameters_schema_uri.

fields.name: serviceAccount
fields.description: The service account that the DeployedModel's container runs as. If not specified, a system generated one will be used, which has minimal permissions and the custom container, if used, may not have enough permission to access other Google Cloud resources. Users deploying the Model must have the `iam.serviceAccounts.actAs` permission on this service account.

fields.name: generateExplanation
fields.description: Generate explanation with the batch prediction results. When set to `true`, the batch prediction output changes based on the `predictions_format` field of the BatchPredictionJob.output_config object: * `bigquery`: output includes a column named `explanation`. The value is a struct that conforms to the Explanation object. * `jsonl`: The JSON objects on each line include an additional entry keyed `explanation`. The value of the entry is a JSON object that conforms to the Explanation object. * `csv`: Generating explanations for CSV format is not supported. If this field is set to true, either the Model.explanation_spec or explanation_spec must be populated.

fields.name: partialFailures
fields.description: Output only. Partial failures encountered. For example, single files that can't be read. This field never exceeds 20 entries. Status details fields contain standard Google Cloud error details.

fields.name: createTime
fields.description: Output only. Time when the BatchPredictionJob was created.

fields.name: startTime
fields.description: Output only. Time when the BatchPredictionJob for the first time entered the `JOB_STATE_RUNNING` state.

fields.name: endTime
fields.description: Output only. Time when the BatchPredictionJob entered any of the following states: `JOB_STATE_SUCCEEDED`, `JOB_STATE_FAILED`, `JOB_STATE_CANCELLED`.

fields.name: updateTime
fields.description: Output only. Time when the BatchPredictionJob was most recently updated.

fields.name: labels
fields.description: The labels with user-defined metadata to organize BatchPredictionJobs. Label keys and values can be no longer than 64 characters (Unicode codepoints), can only contain lowercase letters, numeric characters, underscores and dashes. International characters are allowed. See https://goo.gl/xmQnxf for more information and examples of labels.

fields.name: disableContainerLogging
fields.description: For custom-trained Models and AutoML Tabular Models, the container of the DeployedModel instances will send `stderr` and `stdout` streams to Cloud Logging by default. Please note that the logs incur cost, which are subject to [Cloud Logging pricing](https://cloud.google.com/logging/pricing). User can disable container logging by setting this flag to true.

fields.name: satisfiesPzs
fields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

fields.name: satisfiesPzi
fields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1UnmanagedContainerModel
types.description: Contains model information necessary to perform batch prediction without requiring a full model import.

fields.name: artifactUri
fields.description: The path to the directory containing the Model artifact and any of its supporting files.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1BatchPredictionJobInputConfig
types.description: Configures the input to BatchPredictionJob. See Model.supported_input_storage_formats for Model's supported input formats, and how instances should be expressed via any of them.

fields.name: instancesFormat
fields.description: Required. The format in which instances are given, must be one of the Model's supported_input_storage_formats.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1VertexMultimodalDatasetSource
types.description: The Vertex Multimodal Dataset for the input content.

fields.name: datasetName
fields.description: Required. The resource name of the Vertex Dataset. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/datasets/{dataset}`

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1BatchPredictionJobInstanceConfig
types.description: Configuration defining how to transform batch prediction input instances to the instances that the Model accepts.

fields.name: instanceType
fields.description: The format of the instance that the Model accepts. Vertex AI will convert compatible batch prediction input instance formats to the specified format. Supported values are: * `object`: Each input is converted to JSON object format. * For `bigquery`, each row is converted to an object. * For `jsonl`, each line of the JSONL input must be an object. * Does not apply to `csv`, `file-list`, `tf-record`, or `tf-record-gzip`. * `array`: Each input is converted to JSON array format. * For `bigquery`, each row is converted to an array. The order of columns is determined by the BigQuery column order, unless included_fields is populated. included_fields must be populated for specifying field orders. * For `jsonl`, if each line of the JSONL input is an object, included_fields must be populated for specifying field orders. * Does not apply to `csv`, `file-list`, `tf-record`, or `tf-record-gzip`. If not specified, Vertex AI converts the batch prediction input as follows: * For `bigquery` and `csv`, the behavior is the same as `array`. The order of columns is the same as defined in the file or table, unless included_fields is populated. * For `jsonl`, the prediction instance format is determined by each line of the input. * For `tf-record`/`tf-record-gzip`, each record will be converted to an object in the format of `{"b64": }`, where `` is the Base64-encoded string of the content of the record. * For `file-list`, each file in the list will be converted to an object in the format of `{"b64": }`, where `` is the Base64-encoded string of the content of the file.

fields.name: keyField
fields.description: The name of the field that is considered as a key. The values identified by the key field is not included in the transformed instances that is sent to the Model. This is similar to specifying this name of the field in excluded_fields. In addition, the batch prediction output will not include the instances. Instead the output will only include the value of the key field, in a field named `key` in the output: * For `jsonl` output format, the output will have a `key` field instead of the `instance` field. * For `csv`/`bigquery` output format, the output will have have a `key` column instead of the instance feature columns. The input must be JSONL with objects at each line, CSV, BigQuery or TfRecord.

fields.name: includedFields
fields.description: Fields that will be included in the prediction instance that is sent to the Model. If instance_type is `array`, the order of field names in included_fields also determines the order of the values in the array. When included_fields is populated, excluded_fields must be empty. The input must be JSONL with objects at each line, BigQuery or TfRecord.

fields.name: excludedFields
fields.description: Fields that will be excluded in the prediction instance that is sent to the Model. Excluded will be attached to the batch prediction output if key_field is not specified. When excluded_fields is populated, included_fields must be empty. The input must be JSONL with objects at each line, BigQuery or TfRecord.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1BatchPredictionJobOutputConfig
types.description: Configures the output of BatchPredictionJob. See Model.supported_output_storage_formats for supported output formats, and how predictions are expressed via any of them.

fields.name: predictionsFormat
fields.description: Required. The format in which Vertex AI gives the predictions, must be one of the Model's supported_output_storage_formats.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1VertexMultimodalDatasetDestination
types.description: The details for a Vertex Multimodal Dataset output.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: Optional. Display name of the output dataset.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1BatchDedicatedResources
types.description: A description of resources that are used for performing batch operations, are dedicated to a Model, and need manual configuration.

fields.name: startingReplicaCount
fields.description: Immutable. The number of machine replicas used at the start of the batch operation. If not set, Vertex AI decides starting number, not greater than max_replica_count

fields.name: maxReplicaCount
fields.description: Immutable. The maximum number of machine replicas the batch operation may be scaled to. The default value is 10.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ManualBatchTuningParameters
types.description: Manual batch tuning parameters.

fields.name: batchSize
fields.description: Immutable. The number of the records (e.g. instances) of the operation given in each batch to a machine replica. Machine type, and size of a single record should be considered when setting this parameter, higher value speeds up the batch operation's execution, but too high value will result in a whole batch not fitting in a machine's memory, and the whole operation will fail. The default value is 64.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1BatchPredictionJobOutputInfo
types.description: Further describes this job's output. Supplements output_config.

fields.name: gcsOutputDirectory
fields.description: Output only. The full path of the Cloud Storage directory created, into which the prediction output is written.

fields.name: bigqueryOutputDataset
fields.description: Output only. The path of the BigQuery dataset created, in `bq://projectId.bqDatasetId` format, into which the prediction output is written.

fields.name: vertexMultimodalDatasetName
fields.description: Output only. The resource name of the Vertex Managed Dataset created, into which the prediction output is written. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/datasets/{dataset}`

fields.name: bigqueryOutputTable
fields.description: Output only. The name of the BigQuery table created, in `predictions_` format, into which the prediction output is written. Can be used by UI to generate the BigQuery output path, for example.

types.description: Output only. The detailed state of the job.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ResourcesConsumed
types.description: Statistics information about resource consumption.

fields.name: replicaHours
fields.description: Output only. The number of replica hours used. Note that many replicas may run in parallel, and additionally any given work may be queued for some time. Therefore this value is not strictly related to wall time.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1CompletionStats
types.description: Success and error statistics of processing multiple entities (for example, DataItems or structured data rows) in batch.

fields.name: successfulCount
fields.description: Output only. The number of entities that had been processed successfully.

fields.name: failedCount
fields.description: Output only. The number of entities for which any error was encountered.

fields.name: incompleteCount
fields.description: Output only. In cases when enough errors are encountered a job, pipeline, or operation may be failed as a whole. Below is the number of entities for which the processing had not been finished (either in successful or failed state). Set to -1 if the number is unknown (for example, the operation failed before the total entity number could be collected).

fields.name: successfulForecastPointCount
fields.description: Output only. The number of the successful forecast points that are generated by the forecasting model. This is ONLY used by the forecasting batch prediction.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ModelDeploymentMonitoringJob
types.description: Represents a job that runs periodically to monitor the deployed models in an endpoint. It will analyze the logged training & prediction data to detect any abnormal behaviors.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Output only. Resource name of a ModelDeploymentMonitoringJob.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: Required. The user-defined name of the ModelDeploymentMonitoringJob. The name can be up to 128 characters long and can consist of any UTF-8 characters. Display name of a ModelDeploymentMonitoringJob.

fields.name: endpoint
fields.description: Required. Endpoint resource name. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/endpoints/{endpoint}`

fields.name: modelDeploymentMonitoringObjectiveConfigs
fields.description: Required. The config for monitoring objectives. This is a per DeployedModel config. Each DeployedModel needs to be configured separately.

fields.name: predictInstanceSchemaUri
fields.description: YAML schema file uri describing the format of a single instance, which are given to format this Endpoint's prediction (and explanation). If not set, we will generate predict schema from collected predict requests.

fields.name: samplePredictInstance
fields.description: Sample Predict instance, same format as PredictRequest.instances, this can be set as a replacement of ModelDeploymentMonitoringJob.predict_instance_schema_uri. If not set, we will generate predict schema from collected predict requests.

fields.name: analysisInstanceSchemaUri
fields.description: YAML schema file uri describing the format of a single instance that you want Tensorflow Data Validation (TFDV) to analyze. If this field is empty, all the feature data types are inferred from predict_instance_schema_uri, meaning that TFDV will use the data in the exact format(data type) as prediction request/response. If there are any data type differences between predict instance and TFDV instance, this field can be used to override the schema. For models trained with Vertex AI, this field must be set as all the fields in predict instance formatted as string.

fields.name: bigqueryTables
fields.description: Output only. The created bigquery tables for the job under customer project. Customer could do their own query & analysis. There could be 4 log tables in maximum: 1. Training data logging predict request/response 2. Serving data logging predict request/response

fields.name: logTtl
fields.description: The TTL of BigQuery tables in user projects which stores logs. A day is the basic unit of the TTL and we take the ceil of TTL/86400(a day). e.g. { second: 3600} indicates ttl = 1 day.

fields.name: labels
fields.description: The labels with user-defined metadata to organize your ModelDeploymentMonitoringJob. Label keys and values can be no longer than 64 characters (Unicode codepoints), can only contain lowercase letters, numeric characters, underscores and dashes. International characters are allowed. See https://goo.gl/xmQnxf for more information and examples of labels.

fields.name: createTime
fields.description: Output only. Timestamp when this ModelDeploymentMonitoringJob was created.

fields.name: updateTime
fields.description: Output only. Timestamp when this ModelDeploymentMonitoringJob was updated most recently.

fields.name: nextScheduleTime
fields.description: Output only. Timestamp when this monitoring pipeline will be scheduled to run for the next round.

fields.name: enableMonitoringPipelineLogs
fields.description: If true, the scheduled monitoring pipeline logs are sent to Google Cloud Logging, including pipeline status and anomalies detected. Please note the logs incur cost, which are subject to [Cloud Logging pricing](https://cloud.google.com/logging#pricing).

fields.name: satisfiesPzs
fields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

fields.name: satisfiesPzi
fields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use.

types.name: query_aiplatform_projects_locations_modelDeploymentMonitoringJobs_list_modelDeploymentMonitoringJobs_items_scheduleState
types.description: Output only. Schedule state when the monitoring job is in Running state.

fields.name: targetField

types.name: query_aiplatform_projects_locations_modelDeploymentMonitoringJobs_list_modelDeploymentMonitoringJobs_items_modelDeploymentMonitoringObjectiveConfigs_items_objectiveConfig_trainingPredictionSkewDetectionConfig_skewThresholds
types.description: Key is the feature name and value is the threshold. If a feature needs to be monitored for skew, a value threshold must be configured for that feature. The threshold here is against feature distribution distance between the training and prediction feature.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ThresholdConfig
types.description: The config for feature monitoring threshold.

fields.name: value
fields.description: Specify a threshold value that can trigger the alert. If this threshold config is for feature distribution distance: 1. For categorical feature, the distribution distance is calculated by L-inifinity norm. 2. For numerical feature, the distribution distance is calculated by Jensen–Shannon divergence. Each feature must have a non-zero threshold if they need to be monitored. Otherwise no alert will be triggered for that feature.

types.name: query_aiplatform_projects_locations_modelDeploymentMonitoringJobs_list_modelDeploymentMonitoringJobs_items_modelDeploymentMonitoringObjectiveConfigs_items_objectiveConfig_trainingPredictionSkewDetectionConfig_attributionScoreSkewThresholds
types.description: Key is the feature name and value is the threshold. The threshold here is against attribution score distance between the training and prediction feature.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ThresholdConfig2

fields.name: value
fields.description: Specify a threshold value that can trigger the alert. If this threshold config is for feature distribution distance: 1. For categorical feature, the distribution distance is calculated by L-inifinity norm. 2. For numerical feature, the distribution distance is calculated by Jensen–Shannon divergence. Each feature must have a non-zero threshold if they need to be monitored. Otherwise no alert will be triggered for that feature.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ThresholdConfig3

fields.name: value
fields.description: Specify a threshold value that can trigger the alert. If this threshold config is for feature distribution distance: 1. For categorical feature, the distribution distance is calculated by L-inifinity norm. 2. For numerical feature, the distribution distance is calculated by Jensen–Shannon divergence. Each feature must have a non-zero threshold if they need to be monitored. Otherwise no alert will be triggered for that feature.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ThresholdConfig4

fields.name: value
fields.description: Specify a threshold value that can trigger the alert. If this threshold config is for feature distribution distance: 1. For categorical feature, the distribution distance is calculated by L-inifinity norm. 2. For numerical feature, the distribution distance is calculated by Jensen–Shannon divergence. Each feature must have a non-zero threshold if they need to be monitored. Otherwise no alert will be triggered for that feature.

types.name: query_aiplatform_projects_locations_modelDeploymentMonitoringJobs_list_modelDeploymentMonitoringJobs_items_modelDeploymentMonitoringObjectiveConfigs_items_objectiveConfig_predictionDriftDetectionConfig_attributionScoreDriftThresholds

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ThresholdConfig5

fields.name: value
fields.description: Specify a threshold value that can trigger the alert. If this threshold config is for feature distribution distance: 1. For categorical feature, the distribution distance is calculated by L-inifinity norm. 2. For numerical feature, the distribution distance is calculated by Jensen–Shannon divergence. Each feature must have a non-zero threshold if they need to be monitored. Otherwise no alert will be triggered for that feature.

fields.name: enableFeatureAttributes
fields.description: If want to analyze the Vertex Explainable AI feature attribute scores or not. If set to true, Vertex AI will log the feature attributions from explain response and do the skew/drift detection for them.
