inputFields.description: Output only. Deprecated: This field has no behavior. Use notebook_runtime_type = 'ONE_CLICK' instead. The default template to use if not specified.

inputFields.name: serviceAccount
inputFields.description: Deprecated: This field is ignored and the "Vertex AI Notebook Service Account" (service-PROJECT_NUMBER@gcp-sa-aiplatform-vm.iam.gserviceaccount.com) is used for the runtime workload identity. See https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/service-agents#vertex-ai-notebook-service-account for more details. For NotebookExecutionJob, use NotebookExecutionJob.service_account instead. The service account that the runtime workload runs as. You can use any service account within the same project, but you must have the service account user permission to use the instance. If not specified, the [Compute Engine default service account](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/access/service-accounts#default_service_account) is used.

Label keys and values can be no longer than 64 characters (Unicode codepoints), can only contain lowercase letters, numeric characters, underscores and dashes. International characters are allowed. See https://goo.gl/xmQnxf for more information and examples of labels.

inputFields.description: Required. Duration is accurate to the second. In Notebook, Idle Timeout is accurate to minute so the range of idle_timeout (second) is: 10 * 60 ~ 1440 * 60.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1NotebookEucConfig_Input
types.description: The euc configuration of NotebookRuntimeTemplate.

inputFields.name: eucDisabled
inputFields.description: Input only. Whether EUC is disabled in this NotebookRuntimeTemplate. In proto3, the default value of a boolean is false. In this way, by default EUC will be enabled for NotebookRuntimeTemplate.

inputFields.name: bypassActasCheck
inputFields.description: Output only. Whether ActAs check is bypassed for service account attached to the VM. If false, we need ActAs check for the default Compute Engine Service account. When a Runtime is created, a VM is allocated using Default Compute Engine Service Account. Any user requesting to use this Runtime requires Service Account User (ActAs) permission over this SA. If true, Runtime owner is using EUC and does not require the above permission as VM no longer use default Compute Engine SA, but a P4SA.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ShieldedVmConfig_Input

System reserved label keys are prefixed with "aiplatform.googleapis.com/" and are immutable.

inputFields.name: serviceAccountEmail

inputFields.name: cron
inputFields.description: Cron schedule (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cron) to launch scheduled runs. To explicitly set a timezone to the cron tab, apply a prefix in the cron tab: "CRON_TZ=${IANA_TIME_ZONE}" or "TZ=${IANA_TIME_ZONE}". The ${IANA_TIME_ZONE} may only be a valid string from IANA time zone database. For example, "CRON_TZ=America/New_York 1 * * * *", or "TZ=America/New_York 1 * * * *".

inputFields.name: continuous
inputFields.description: Optional. If true, syncs the FeatureView in a continuous manner to Online Store.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1FeatureViewIndexConfig_Input
types.description: Configuration for vector indexing.

inputFields.name: bruteForceConfig
inputFields.description: Configuration options for using brute force search.

inputFields.name: embeddingColumn
inputFields.description: Optional. Column of embedding. This column contains the source data to create index for vector search. embedding_column must be set when using vector search.

inputFields.name: filterColumns
inputFields.description: Optional. Columns of features that're used to filter vector search results.

inputFields.name: crowdingColumn
inputFields.description: Optional. Column of crowding. This column contains crowding attribute which is a constraint on a neighbor list produced by FeatureOnlineStoreService.SearchNearestEntities to diversify search results. If NearestNeighborQuery.per_crowding_attribute_neighbor_count is set to K in SearchNearestEntitiesRequest, it's guaranteed that no more than K entities of the same crowding attribute are returned in the response.

inputFields.name: embeddingDimension
inputFields.description: Optional. The number of dimensions of the input embedding.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1FeatureViewIndexConfigTreeAHConfig_Input
types.description: Configuration options for the tree-AH algorithm.

inputFields.name: leafNodeEmbeddingCount
inputFields.description: Optional. Number of embeddings on each leaf node. The default value is 1000 if not set.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1FeatureViewOptimizedConfig_Input
types.description: Configuration for FeatureViews created in Optimized FeatureOnlineStore.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1AutomaticResources_Input
types.description: A description of resources that to large degree are decided by Vertex AI, and require only a modest additional configuration. Each Model supporting these resources documents its specific guidelines.

inputFields.name: minReplicaCount
inputFields.description: Immutable. The minimum number of replicas that will be always deployed on. If traffic against it increases, it may dynamically be deployed onto more replicas up to max_replica_count, and as traffic decreases, some of these extra replicas may be freed. If the requested value is too large, the deployment will error.

inputFields.name: maxReplicaCount
inputFields.description: Immutable. The maximum number of replicas that may be deployed on when the traffic against it increases. If the requested value is too large, the deployment will error, but if deployment succeeds then the ability to scale to that many replicas is guaranteed (barring service outages). If traffic increases beyond what its replicas at maximum may handle, a portion of the traffic will be dropped. If this value is not provided, a no upper bound for scaling under heavy traffic will be assume, though Vertex AI may be unable to scale beyond certain replica number.

types.description: Nearest neighbors for one query.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1SearchNearestEntitiesRequest_Input
types.description: The request message for FeatureOnlineStoreService.SearchNearestEntities.

inputFields.name: returnFullEntity
inputFields.description: Optional. If set to true, the full entities (including all vector values and metadata) of the nearest neighbors are returned; otherwise only entity id of the nearest neighbors will be returned. Note that returning full entities will significantly increase the latency and cost of the query.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1NearestNeighborQuery_Input
types.description: A query to find a number of similar entities.

inputFields.name: entityId
inputFields.description: Optional. The entity id whose similar entities should be searched for. If embedding is set, search will use embedding instead of entity_id.

inputFields.name: neighborCount
inputFields.description: Optional. The number of similar entities to be retrieved from feature view for each query.

inputFields.name: stringFilters
inputFields.description: Optional. The list of string filters.

inputFields.name: numericFilters
inputFields.description: Optional. The list of numeric filters.

inputFields.name: perCrowdingAttributeNeighborCount
inputFields.description: Optional. Crowding is a constraint on a neighbor list produced by nearest neighbor search requiring that no more than sper_crowding_attribute_neighbor_count of the k neighbors returned have the same value of crowding_attribute. It's used for improving result diversity.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1NearestNeighborQueryEmbedding_Input
types.description: The embedding vector.

inputFields.name: value
inputFields.description: Optional. Individual value in the embedding.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1NearestNeighborQueryStringFilter_Input
types.description: String filter is used to search a subset of the entities by using boolean rules on string columns. For example: if a query specifies string filter with 'name = color, allow_tokens = {red, blue}, deny_tokens = {purple}',' then that query will match entities that are red or blue, but if those points are also purple, then they will be excluded even if they are red/blue. Only string filter is supported for now, numeric filter will be supported in the near future.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Required. Column names in BigQuery that used as filters.

inputFields.name: allowTokens
inputFields.description: Optional. The allowed tokens.

inputFields.name: denyTokens
inputFields.description: Optional. The denied tokens.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1NearestNeighborQueryNumericFilter_Input
types.description: Numeric filter is used to search a subset of the entities by using boolean rules on numeric columns. For example: Database Point 0: {name: "a" value_int: 42} {name: "b" value_float: 1.0} Database Point 1: {name: "a" value_int: 10} {name: "b" value_float: 2.0} Database Point 2: {name: "a" value_int: -1} {name: "b" value_float: 3.0} Query: {name: "a" value_int: 12 operator: LESS} // Matches Point 1, 2 {name: "b" value_float: 2.0 operator: EQUAL} // Matches Point 1

inputFields.name: valueInt
inputFields.description: int value type.

inputFields.name: valueFloat
inputFields.description: float value type.

inputFields.name: valueDouble
inputFields.description: double value type.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Required. Column name in BigQuery that used as filters.

types.name: mutationInput_aiplatform_projects_locations_featureOnlineStores_featureViews_searchNearestEntities_input_query_numericFilters_items_op
types.description: Optional. This MUST be specified for queries and must NOT be specified for database points.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1NearestNeighborQueryParameters_Input
types.description: Parameters that can be overrided in each query to tune query latency and recall.

inputFields.name: approximateNeighborCandidates
inputFields.description: Optional. The number of neighbors to find via approximate search before exact reordering is performed; if set, this value must be > neighbor_count.

inputFields.name: leafNodesSearchFraction
inputFields.description: Optional. The fraction of the number of leaves to search, set at query time allows user to tune search performance. This value increase result in both search accuracy and latency increase. The value should be between 0.0 and 1.0.

Label keys and values can be no longer than 64 characters (Unicode codepoints), can only contain lowercase letters, numeric characters, underscores and dashes. International characters are allowed. See https://goo.gl/xmQnxf for more information on and examples of labels. No more than 64 user labels can be associated with one FeatureGroup(System labels are excluded)." types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1FeatureGroupBigQuery_Input
types.description: Input source type for BigQuery Tables and Views.

inputFields.name: entityIdColumns
inputFields.description: Optional. Columns to construct entity_id / row keys. If not provided defaults to `entity_id`.

inputFields.name: staticDataSource
inputFields.description: Optional. Set if the data source is not a time-series.

inputFields.name: dense
inputFields.description: Optional. If set, all feature values will be fetched from a single row per unique entityId including nulls. If not set, will collapse all rows for each unique entityId into a singe row with any non-null values if present, if no non-null values are present will sync null. ex: If source has schema `(entity_id, feature_timestamp, f0, f1)` and the following rows: `(e1, 2020-01-01T10:00:00.123Z, 10, 15)` `(e1, 2020-02-01T10:00:00.123Z, 20, null)` If dense is set, `(e1, 20, null)` is synced to online stores. If dense is not set, `(e1, 20, 15)` is synced to online stores.

inputFields.name: timestampColumn
inputFields.description: Optional. Column hosting timestamp values for a time-series source. Will be used to determine the latest `feature_values` for each entity. Optional. If not provided, column named `feature_timestamp` of type `TIMESTAMP` will be used.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ImportDataRequest_Input
types.description: Request message for DatasetService.ImportData.

inputFields.name: importConfigs
inputFields.description: Required. The desired input locations. The contents of all input locations will be imported in one batch.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ImportDataConfig_Input
types.description: Describes the location from where we import data into a Dataset, together with the labels that will be applied to the DataItems and the Annotations.

inputFields.name: dataItemLabels
inputFields.description: Labels that will be applied to newly imported DataItems. If an identical DataItem as one being imported already exists in the Dataset, then these labels will be appended to these of the already existing one, and if labels with identical key is imported before, the old label value will be overwritten. If two DataItems are identical in the same import data operation, the labels will be combined and if key collision happens in this case, one of the values will be picked randomly. Two DataItems are considered identical if their content bytes are identical (e.g. image bytes or pdf bytes). These labels will be overridden by Annotation labels specified inside index file referenced by import_schema_uri, e.g. jsonl file.

inputFields.name: annotationLabels
inputFields.description: Labels that will be applied to newly imported Annotations. If two Annotations are identical, one of them will be deduped. Two Annotations are considered identical if their payload, payload_schema_uri and all of their labels are the same. These labels will be overridden by Annotation labels specified inside index file referenced by import_schema_uri, e.g. jsonl file.

inputFields.name: importSchemaUri
inputFields.description: Required. Points to a YAML file stored on Google Cloud Storage describing the import format. Validation will be done against the schema. The schema is defined as an [OpenAPI 3.0.2 Schema Object](https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/main/versions/3.0.2.md#schemaObject).

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ExportDataRequest_Input
types.description: Request message for DatasetService.ExportData.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ExportDataConfig_Input
types.description: Describes what part of the Dataset is to be exported, the destination of the export and how to export.

inputFields.name: annotationsFilter
inputFields.description: An expression for filtering what part of the Dataset is to be exported. Only Annotations that match this filter will be exported. The filter syntax is the same as in ListAnnotations.

inputFields.name: savedQueryId
inputFields.description: The ID of a SavedQuery (annotation set) under the Dataset specified by ExportDataRequest.name used for filtering Annotations for training. Only used for custom training data export use cases. Only applicable to Datasets that have SavedQueries. Only Annotations that are associated with this SavedQuery are used in respectively training. When used in conjunction with annotations_filter, the Annotations used for training are filtered by both saved_query_id and annotations_filter. Only one of saved_query_id and annotation_schema_uri should be specified as both of them represent the same thing: problem type.

inputFields.name: annotationSchemaUri
inputFields.description: The Cloud Storage URI that points to a YAML file describing the annotation schema. The schema is defined as an OpenAPI 3.0.2 [Schema Object](https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/main/versions/3.0.2.md#schemaObject). The schema files that can be used here are found in gs://google-cloud-aiplatform/schema/dataset/annotation/, note that the chosen schema must be consistent with metadata of the Dataset specified by ExportDataRequest.name. Only used for custom training data export use cases. Only applicable to Datasets that have DataItems and Annotations. Only Annotations that both match this schema and belong to DataItems not ignored by the split method are used in respectively training, validation or test role, depending on the role of the DataItem they are on. When used in conjunction with annotations_filter, the Annotations used for training are filtered by both annotations_filter and annotation_schema_uri.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ExportFractionSplit_Input
types.description: Assigns the input data to training, validation, and test sets as per the given fractions. Any of `training_fraction`, `validation_fraction` and `test_fraction` may optionally be provided, they must sum to up to 1. If the provided ones sum to less than 1, the remainder is assigned to sets as decided by Vertex AI. If none of the fractions are set, by default roughly 80% of data is used for training, 10% for validation, and 10% for test.

inputFields.name: trainingFraction
inputFields.description: The fraction of the input data that is to be used to train the Model.

inputFields.name: validationFraction
inputFields.description: The fraction of the input data that is to be used to validate the Model.

inputFields.name: testFraction
inputFields.description: The fraction of the input data that is to be used to evaluate the Model.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ExportFilterSplit_Input
types.description: Assigns input data to training, validation, and test sets based on the given filters, data pieces not matched by any filter are ignored. Currently only supported for Datasets containing DataItems. If any of the filters in this message are to match nothing, then they can be set as '-' (the minus sign). Supported only for unstructured Datasets.

inputFields.name: trainingFilter
inputFields.description: Required. A filter on DataItems of the Dataset. DataItems that match this filter are used to train the Model. A filter with same syntax as the one used in DatasetService.ListDataItems may be used. If a single DataItem is matched by more than one of the FilterSplit filters, then it is assigned to the first set that applies to it in the training, validation, test order.

inputFields.name: validationFilter
inputFields.description: Required. A filter on DataItems of the Dataset. DataItems that match this filter are used to validate the Model. A filter with same syntax as the one used in DatasetService.ListDataItems may be used. If a single DataItem is matched by more than one of the FilterSplit filters, then it is assigned to the first set that applies to it in the training, validation, test order.

inputFields.name: testFilter
inputFields.description: Required. A filter on DataItems of the Dataset. DataItems that match this filter are used to test the Model. A filter with same syntax as the one used in DatasetService.ListDataItems may be used. If a single DataItem is matched by more than one of the FilterSplit filters, then it is assigned to the first set that applies to it in the training, validation, test order.

types.name: GoogleApiHttpBody
types.description: Message that represents an arbitrary HTTP body. It should only be used for payload formats that can't be represented as JSON, such as raw binary or an HTML page. This message can be used both in streaming and non-streaming API methods in the request as well as the response. It can be used as a top-level request field, which is convenient if one wants to extract parameters from either the URL or HTTP template into the request fields and also want access to the raw HTTP body. Example: message GetResourceRequest { // A unique request id. string request_id = 1; // The raw HTTP body is bound to this field. google.api.HttpBody http_body = 2; } service ResourceService { rpc GetResource(GetResourceRequest) returns (google.api.HttpBody); rpc UpdateResource(google.api.HttpBody) returns (google.protobuf.Empty); } Example with streaming methods: service CaldavService { rpc GetCalendar(stream google.api.HttpBody) returns (stream google.api.HttpBody); rpc UpdateCalendar(stream google.api.HttpBody) returns (stream google.api.HttpBody); } Use of this type only changes how the request and response bodies are handled, all other features will continue to work unchanged.

fields.name: contentType
fields.description: The HTTP Content-Type header value specifying the content type of the body.

fields.name: data
fields.description: The HTTP request/response body as raw binary.

fields.name: extensions
fields.description: Application specific response metadata. Must be set in the first response for streaming APIs.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ReasoningEngine_Input
types.description: ReasoningEngine provides a customizable runtime for models to determine which actions to take and in which order.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Identifier. The resource name of the ReasoningEngine. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/reasoningEngines/{reasoning_engine}`

inputFields.name: displayName
inputFields.description: Required. The display name of the ReasoningEngine.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: Optional. The description of the ReasoningEngine.

inputFields.name: createTime
inputFields.description: Output only. Timestamp when this ReasoningEngine was created.

inputFields.name: updateTime
inputFields.description: Output only. Timestamp when this ReasoningEngine was most recently updated.

inputFields.name: etag
inputFields.description: Optional. Used to perform consistent read-modify-write updates. If not set, a blind "overwrite" update happens.

inputFields.name: labels
inputFields.description: Labels for the ReasoningEngine.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ReasoningEngineSpec_Input
types.description: ReasoningEngine configurations

inputFields.name: serviceAccount
inputFields.description: Optional. The service account that the Reasoning Engine artifact runs as. It should have "roles/storage.objectViewer" for reading the user project's Cloud Storage and "roles/aiplatform.user" for using Vertex extensions. If not specified, the Vertex AI Reasoning Engine Service Agent in the project will be used.

inputFields.name: classMethods
inputFields.description: Optional. Declarations for object class methods in OpenAPI specification format.

inputFields.name: agentFramework
inputFields.description: Optional. The OSS agent framework used to develop the agent. Currently supported values: "google-adk", "langchain", "langgraph", "ag2", "llama-index", "custom".

inputFields.name: effectiveIdentity
inputFields.description: Output only. The identity to use for the Reasoning Engine. It can contain one of the following values: * service-{project}@gcp-sa-aiplatform-re.googleapis.com (for SERVICE_AGENT identity type) * {name}@{project}.gserviceaccount.com (for SERVICE_ACCOUNT identity type) * agents.global.{org}.system.id.goog/resources/aiplatform/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/reasoningEngines/{reasoning_engine} (for AGENT_IDENTITY identity type)

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ReasoningEngineSpecSourceCodeSpec_Input
types.description: Specification for deploying from source code.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ReasoningEngineSpecSourceCodeSpecInlineSource_Input
types.description: Specifies source code provided as a byte stream.

inputFields.name: sourceArchive
inputFields.description: Required. Input only. The application source code archive. It must be a compressed tarball (.tar.gz) file.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ReasoningEngineSpecSourceCodeSpecDeveloperConnectSource_Input
types.description: Specifies source code to be fetched from a Git repository managed through the Developer Connect service.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ReasoningEngineSpecSourceCodeSpecDeveloperConnectConfig_Input
types.description: Specifies the configuration for fetching source code from a Git repository that is managed by Developer Connect. This includes the repository, revision, and directory to use.

inputFields.name: gitRepositoryLink
inputFields.description: Required. The Developer Connect Git repository link, formatted as `projects/*/locations/*/connections/*/gitRepositoryLink/*`.

inputFields.name: dir
inputFields.description: Required. Directory, relative to the source root, in which to run the build.

inputFields.name: revision
inputFields.description: Required. The revision to fetch from the Git repository such as a branch, a tag, a commit SHA, or any Git ref.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ReasoningEngineSpecSourceCodeSpecPythonSpec_Input
types.description: Specification for running a Python application from source.

inputFields.name: version
inputFields.description: Optional. The version of Python to use. Support version includes 3.9, 3.10, 3.11, 3.12, 3.13, 3.14. If not specified, default value is 3.10.

inputFields.name: entrypointModule
inputFields.description: Optional. The Python module to load as the entrypoint, specified as a fully qualified module name. For example: path.to.agent. If not specified, defaults to "agent". The project root will be added to Python sys.path, allowing imports to be specified relative to the root. This field should not be set if the source is `agent_config_source`.

inputFields.name: entrypointObject
inputFields.description: Optional. The name of the callable object within the `entrypoint_module` to use as the application If not specified, defaults to "root_agent". This field should not be set if the source is `agent_config_source`.

inputFields.name: requirementsFile
inputFields.description: Optional. The path to the requirements file, relative to the source root. If not specified, defaults to "requirements.txt".

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ReasoningEngineSpecSourceCodeSpecImageSpec_Input
types.description: The image spec for building an image (within a single build step), based on the config file (i.e. Dockerfile) in the source directory.

inputFields.name: buildArgs
inputFields.description: Optional. Build arguments to be used. They will be passed through --build-arg flags.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ReasoningEngineSpecContainerSpec_Input
types.description: Specification for deploying from a container image.

inputFields.name: imageUri
inputFields.description: Required. The Artifact Registry Docker image URI (e.g., us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/my-project/my-repo/my-image:tag) of the container image that is to be run on each worker replica.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ReasoningEngineSpecPackageSpec_Input
types.description: User-provided package specification, containing pickled object and package requirements.

inputFields.name: pickleObjectGcsUri
inputFields.description: Optional. The Cloud Storage URI of the pickled python object.

inputFields.name: dependencyFilesGcsUri
inputFields.description: Optional. The Cloud Storage URI of the dependency files in tar.gz format.

inputFields.name: requirementsGcsUri
inputFields.description: Optional. The Cloud Storage URI of the `requirements.txt` file

inputFields.name: pythonVersion
inputFields.description: Optional. The Python version. Supported values are 3.9, 3.10, 3.11, 3.12, 3.13, 3.14. If not specified, the default value is 3.10.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ReasoningEngineSpecDeploymentSpec_Input
types.description: The specification of a Reasoning Engine deployment.

inputFields.name: env
inputFields.description: Optional. Environment variables to be set with the Reasoning Engine deployment. The environment variables can be updated through the UpdateReasoningEngine API.

inputFields.name: secretEnv
inputFields.description: Optional. Environment variables where the value is a secret in Cloud Secret Manager. To use this feature, add 'Secret Manager Secret Accessor' role (roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor) to AI Platform Reasoning Engine Service Agent.

inputFields.name: minInstances
inputFields.description: Optional. The minimum number of application instances that will be kept running at all times. Defaults to 1. Range: [0, 10].

inputFields.name: maxInstances
inputFields.description: Optional. The maximum number of application instances that can be launched to handle increased traffic. Defaults to 100. Range: [1, 1000]. If VPC-SC or PSC-I is enabled, the acceptable range is [1, 100].

inputFields.name: resourceLimits
inputFields.description: Optional. Resource limits for each container. Only 'cpu' and 'memory' keys are supported. Defaults to {"cpu": "4", "memory": "4Gi"}. * The only supported values for CPU are '1', '2', '4', '6' and '8'. For more information, go to https://cloud.google.com/run/docs/configuring/cpu. * The only supported values for memory are '1Gi', '2Gi', ... '32 Gi'. * For required cpu on different memory values, go to https://cloud.google.com/run/docs/configuring/memory-limits

inputFields.name: containerConcurrency
inputFields.description: Optional. Concurrency for each container and agent server. Recommended value: 2 * cpu + 1. Defaults to 9.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1SecretEnvVar_Input
types.description: Represents an environment variable where the value is a secret in Cloud Secret Manager.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Required. Name of the secret environment variable.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1SecretRef_Input
types.description: Reference to a secret stored in the Cloud Secret Manager that will provide the value for this environment variable.

inputFields.name: secret
inputFields.description: Required. The name of the secret in Cloud Secret Manager. Format: {secret_name}.

inputFields.name: version
inputFields.description: The Cloud Secret Manager secret version. Can be 'latest' for the latest version, an integer for a specific version, or a version alias.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1PscInterfaceConfig_Input
types.description: Configuration for PSC-I.

inputFields.name: networkAttachment
inputFields.description: Optional. The name of the Compute Engine [network attachment](https://cloud.google.com/vpc/docs/about-network-attachments) to attach to the resource within the region and user project. To specify this field, you must have already [created a network attachment] (https://cloud.google.com/vpc/docs/create-manage-network-attachments#create-network-attachments). This field is only used for resources using PSC-I.

inputFields.name: dnsPeeringConfigs
inputFields.description: Optional. DNS peering configurations. When specified, Vertex AI will attempt to configure DNS peering zones in the tenant project VPC to resolve the specified domains using the target network's Cloud DNS. The user must grant the dns.peer role to the Vertex AI Service Agent on the target project.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1DnsPeeringConfig_Input
types.description: DNS peering configuration. These configurations are used to create DNS peering zones in the Vertex tenant project VPC, enabling resolution of records within the specified domain hosted in the target network's Cloud DNS.

inputFields.name: domain
inputFields.description: Required. The DNS name suffix of the zone being peered to, e.g., "my-internal-domain.corp.". Must end with a dot.

inputFields.name: targetProject
inputFields.description: Required. The project ID hosting the Cloud DNS managed zone that contains the 'domain'. The Vertex AI Service Agent requires the dns.peer role on this project.

inputFields.name: targetNetwork
inputFields.description: Required. The VPC network name in the target_project where the DNS zone specified by 'domain' is visible.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ReasoningEngineContextSpec_Input
types.description: Configuration for how Agent Engine sub-resources should manage context.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ReasoningEngineContextSpecMemoryBankConfig_Input
types.description: Specification for a Memory Bank.

inputFields.name: customizationConfigs
inputFields.description: Optional. Configuration for how to customize Memory Bank behavior for a particular scope.

inputFields.name: disableMemoryRevisions
inputFields.description: If true, no memory revisions will be created for any requests to the Memory Bank.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ReasoningEngineContextSpecMemoryBankConfigGenerationConfig_Input
types.description: Configuration for how to generate memories.

inputFields.name: model
inputFields.description: Required. The model used to generate memories. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/publishers/google/models/{model}`.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ReasoningEngineContextSpecMemoryBankConfigSimilaritySearchConfig_Input
types.description: Configuration for how to perform similarity search on memories.

inputFields.name: embeddingModel
inputFields.description: Required. The model used to generate embeddings to lookup similar memories. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/publishers/google/models/{model}`.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1MemoryBankCustomizationConfig_Input
types.description: Represents configuration for organizing memories for a particular scope.

inputFields.name: scopeKeys
inputFields.description: Optional. The scope keys (i.e. 'user_id') for which to use this config. A request's scope must include all of the provided keys for the config to be used (order does not matter). If empty, then the config will be used for all requests that do not have a more specific config. Only one default config is allowed per Memory Bank.

inputFields.name: memoryTopics
inputFields.description: Optional. Topics of information that should be extracted from conversations and stored as memories. If not set, then Memory Bank's default topics will be used.

inputFields.name: generateMemoriesExamples
inputFields.description: Optional. Examples of how to generate memories for a particular scope.

inputFields.name: enableThirdPersonMemories
inputFields.description: Optional. If true, then the memories will be generated in the third person (i.e. "The user generates memories with Memory Bank."). By default, the memories will be generated in the first person (i.e. "I generate memories with Memory Bank.")

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1MemoryBankCustomizationConfigMemoryTopic_Input
types.description: A topic of information that should be extracted from conversations and stored as memories.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1MemoryBankCustomizationConfigMemoryTopicCustomMemoryTopic_Input
types.description: A custom memory topic defined by the developer.

inputFields.name: label
inputFields.description: Required. The label of the topic.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: Required. Description of the memory topic. This should explain what information should be extracted for this topic.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1MemoryBankCustomizationConfigMemoryTopicManagedMemoryTopic_Input
types.description: A managed memory topic defined by the system.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1MemoryBankCustomizationConfigGenerateMemoriesExample_Input
types.description: An example of how to generate memories for a particular scope.

inputFields.name: generatedMemories
inputFields.description: Optional. The memories that are expected to be generated from the input conversation. An empty list indicates that no memories are expected to be generated for the input conversation.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1MemoryBankCustomizationConfigGenerateMemoriesExampleConversationSource_Input
types.description: A conversation source for the example. This is similar to `DirectContentsSource`.

inputFields.name: events
inputFields.description: Optional. The input conversation events for the example.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1MemoryBankCustomizationConfigGenerateMemoriesExampleConversationSourceEvent_Input
types.description: A single conversation event.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1MemoryBankCustomizationConfigGenerateMemoriesExampleGeneratedMemory_Input
types.description: A memory generated by the operation.

inputFields.name: fact
inputFields.description: Required. The fact to generate a memory from.

inputFields.name: topics
inputFields.description: Optional. The list of topics that the memory should be associated with. For example, use `custom_memory_topic_label = "jargon"` if the extracted memory is an example of memory extraction for the custom topic `jargon`.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1MemoryTopicId_Input
types.description: A memory topic identifier. This will be used to label a Memory and to restrict which topics are eligible for generation or retrieval.

inputFields.name: customMemoryTopicLabel
inputFields.description: Optional. The custom memory topic label.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1MemoryBankCustomizationConfigConsolidationConfig_Input
types.description: Represents configuration for customizing how memories are consolidated.

inputFields.name: revisionsPerCandidateCount
inputFields.description: Optional. The maximum number of revisions to consider for each candidate memory. If not set, then the default value (1) will be used, which means that only the latest revision will be considered.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ReasoningEngineContextSpecMemoryBankConfigTtlConfig_Input
types.description: Configuration for automatically setting the TTL ("time-to-live") of the memories in the Memory Bank.

inputFields.name: defaultTtl
inputFields.description: Optional. The default TTL duration of the memories in the Memory Bank. This applies to all operations that create or update a memory.

inputFields.name: memoryRevisionDefaultTtl
inputFields.description: Optional. The default TTL duration of the memory revisions in the Memory Bank. This applies to all operations that create a memory revision. If not set, a default TTL of 365 days will be used.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1ReasoningEngineContextSpecMemoryBankConfigTtlConfigGranularTtlConfig_Input
types.description: Configuration for TTL of the memories in the Memory Bank based on the action that created or updated the memory.

inputFields.name: createTtl
inputFields.description: Optional. The TTL duration for memories uploaded via CreateMemory.

inputFields.name: generateCreatedTtl
inputFields.description: Optional. The TTL duration for memories newly generated via GenerateMemories (GenerateMemoriesResponse.GeneratedMemory.Action.CREATED).

inputFields.name: generateUpdatedTtl
inputFields.description: Optional. The TTL duration for memories updated via GenerateMemories (GenerateMemoriesResponse.GeneratedMemory.Action.UPDATED). In the case of an UPDATE action, the `expire_time` of the existing memory will be updated to the new value (now + TTL).

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1Memory_Input
types.description: A memory.

inputFields.name: expireTime
inputFields.description: Optional. Timestamp of when this resource is considered expired. This is *always* provided on output when `expiration` is set on input, regardless of whether `expire_time` or `ttl` was provided.

inputFields.name: ttl
inputFields.description: Optional. Input only. The TTL for this resource. The expiration time is computed: now + TTL.

inputFields.name: revisionExpireTime
inputFields.description: Optional. Input only. Timestamp of when the revision is considered expired. If not set, the memory revision will be kept until manually deleted.

inputFields.name: revisionTtl
inputFields.description: Optional. Input only. The TTL for the revision. The expiration time is computed: now + TTL.

inputFields.name: disableMemoryRevisions
inputFields.description: Optional. Input only. If true, no revision will be created for this request.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Identifier. The resource name of the Memory. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/reasoningEngines/{reasoning_engine}/memories/{memory}`

inputFields.name: displayName
inputFields.description: Optional. Display name of the Memory.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: Optional. Description of the Memory.

inputFields.name: createTime
inputFields.description: Output only. Timestamp when this Memory was created.

inputFields.name: updateTime
inputFields.description: Output only. Timestamp when this Memory was most recently updated.

inputFields.name: fact
inputFields.description: Required. Semantic knowledge extracted from the source content.

inputFields.name: scope
inputFields.description: Required. Immutable. The scope of the Memory. Memories are isolated within their scope. The scope is defined when creating or generating memories. Scope values cannot contain the wildcard character '*'.

inputFields.name: topics
inputFields.description: Optional. The Topics of the Memory.

inputFields.name: revisionLabels
inputFields.description: Optional. Input only. The labels to apply to the Memory Revision created as a result of this request.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GenerateMemoriesRequest_Input
types.description: Request message for MemoryBankService.GenerateMemories. Maximum size is 8 MB.

inputFields.name: revisionExpireTime
inputFields.description: Optional. Timestamp of when the revision is considered expired. If not set, the memory revision will be kept until manually deleted.

inputFields.name: revisionTtl
inputFields.description: Optional. The TTL for the revision. The expiration time is computed: now + TTL.

inputFields.name: disableMemoryRevisions
inputFields.description: Optional. If true, no revisions will be created for this request.

inputFields.name: disableConsolidation
inputFields.description: Optional. If true, generated memories will not be consolidated with existing memories; all generated memories will be added as new memories regardless of whether they are duplicates of or contradictory to existing memories. By default, memory consolidation is enabled.

inputFields.name: scope
inputFields.description: Optional. The scope of the memories that should be generated. Memories will be consolidated across memories with the same scope. Must be provided unless the scope is defined in the source content. If `scope` is provided, it will override the scope defined in the source content. Scope values cannot contain the wildcard character '*'.

inputFields.name: revisionLabels
inputFields.description: Optional. Labels to be applied to the generated memory revisions. For example, you can use this to label a revision with its data source.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GenerateMemoriesRequestVertexSessionSource_Input
types.description: Defines an Agent Engine Session from which to generate the memories. If `scope` is not provided, the scope will be extracted from the Session (i.e. {"user_id": sesison.user_id}).

inputFields.name: session
inputFields.description: Required. The resource name of the Session to generate memories for. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/reasoningEngines/{reasoning_engine}/sessions/{session}`

inputFields.name: startTime
inputFields.description: Optional. Time range to define which session events should be used to generate memories. Start time (inclusive) of the time range. If not set, the start time is unbounded.

inputFields.name: endTime
inputFields.description: Optional. End time (exclusive) of the time range. If not set, the end time is unbounded.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GenerateMemoriesRequestDirectContentsSource_Input
types.description: Defines a direct source of content from which to generate the memories.

inputFields.name: events
inputFields.description: Required. The source content (i.e. chat history) to generate memories from.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GenerateMemoriesRequestDirectContentsSourceEvent_Input
types.description: A single piece of conversation from which to generate memories.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GenerateMemoriesRequestDirectMemoriesSource_Input

inputFields.name: directMemories
inputFields.description: Required. The direct memories to upload to Memory Bank. At most 5 direct memories are allowed per request.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1GenerateMemoriesRequestDirectMemoriesSourceDirectMemory_Input
types.description: A direct memory to upload to Memory Bank.

inputFields.name: fact
inputFields.description: Required. The fact to consolidate with existing memories.

inputFields.name: topics
inputFields.description: Optional. The topics that the consolidated memories should be associated with.

types.name: mutationInput_aiplatform_projects_locations_reasoningEngines_memories_generate_input_metadataMergeStrategy

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1RetrieveMemoriesResponse
types.description: Response message for MemoryBankService.RetrieveMemories.

fields.name: retrievedMemories
fields.description: The retrieved memories.

fields.name: nextPageToken
fields.description: A token that can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page. If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages. This token is not set if similarity search was used for retrieval.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1RetrieveMemoriesResponseRetrievedMemory
types.description: A retrieved memory.

fields.name: distance
fields.description: The distance between the query and the retrieved Memory. Smaller values indicate more similar memories. This is only set if similarity search was used for retrieval.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1RetrieveMemoriesRequest_Input
types.description: Request message for MemoryBankService.RetrieveMemories.

inputFields.name: scope
inputFields.description: Required. The scope of the memories to retrieve. A memory must have exactly the same scope (`Memory.scope`) as the scope provided here to be retrieved (same keys and values). Order does not matter, but it is case-sensitive.

inputFields.name: filter
inputFields.description: Optional. The standard list filter that will be applied to the retrieved memories. More detail in [AIP-160](https://google.aip.dev/160). Supported fields: * `fact` * `create_time` * `update_time` * `topics` (i.e. `topics.custom_memory_topic_label: "example topic" OR topics.managed_memory_topic: USER_PREFERENCES`)

inputFields.name: filterGroups
inputFields.description: Optional. Metadata filters that will be applied to the retrieved memories' `metadata` using OR logic. Filters are defined using disjunctive normal form (OR of ANDs). For example: `filter_groups: [{filters: [{key: "author", value: {string_value: "agent 123"}, op: EQUAL}]}, {filters: [{key: "label", value: {string_value: "travel"}, op: EQUAL}, {key: "author", value: {string_value: "agent 321"}, op: EQUAL}]}]` would be equivalent to the logical expression: `(metadata.author = "agent 123" OR (metadata.label = "travel" AND metadata.author = "agent 321"))`.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1RetrieveMemoriesRequestSimilaritySearchParams_Input
types.description: Parameters for semantic similarity search based retrieval.

inputFields.name: searchQuery
inputFields.description: Required. Query to use for similarity search retrieval. If provided, then the parent ReasoningEngine must have ReasoningEngineContextSpec.MemoryBankConfig.SimilaritySearchConfig set.

inputFields.name: topK
inputFields.description: Optional. The maximum number of memories to return. The service may return fewer than this value. If unspecified, at most 3 memories will be returned. The maximum value is 100; values above 100 will be coerced to 100.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1RetrieveMemoriesRequestSimpleRetrievalParams_Input
types.description: Parameters for simple (non-similarity search) retrieval.

inputFields.name: pageSize
inputFields.description: Optional. The maximum number of memories to return. The service may return fewer than this value. If unspecified, at most 3 memories will be returned. The maximum value is 100; values above 100 will be coerced to 100.

inputFields.name: pageToken
inputFields.description: Optional. A page token, received from a previous `RetrieveMemories` call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1MemoryConjunctionFilter_Input
types.description: A conjunction of filters that will be combined using AND logic.

inputFields.name: filters
inputFields.description: Filters that will combined using AND logic.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1MemoryFilter_Input
types.description: Filter to apply when retrieving memories.

inputFields.name: key
inputFields.description: Key of the filter. For example, "author" would apply to `metadata` entries with the key "author".

inputFields.name: negate
inputFields.description: If true, the filter will be negated.

types.name: mutationInput_aiplatform_projects_locations_reasoningEngines_memories_retrieve_input_filterGroups_items_filters_items_op
types.description: Operator to apply to the filter. If not set, then EQUAL will be used.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1MemoryMetadataValue_Input3
types.description: Memory metadata.

inputFields.name: stringValue
inputFields.description: String value.

inputFields.name: doubleValue
inputFields.description: Double value.

inputFields.name: boolValue
inputFields.description: Boolean value.

inputFields.name: timestampValue
inputFields.description: Timestamp value. When filtering on timestamp values, only the seconds field will be compared.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1RollbackMemoryRequest_Input
types.description: Request message for MemoryBankService.RollbackMemory.

inputFields.name: targetRevisionId
inputFields.description: Required. The ID of the revision to rollback to.

types.name: GoogleCloudAiplatformV1PurgeMemoriesRequest_Input
types.description: Request message for MemoryBankService.PurgeMemories.

inputFields.name: filter
inputFields.description: Required. The standard list filter to determine which memories to purge. More detail in [AIP-160](https://google.aip.dev/160).

inputFields.name: force
inputFields.description: Optional. If true, the memories will actually be purged. If false, the purge request will be validated but not executed.

inputFields.name: filterGroups
inputFields.description: Optional. Metadata filters that will be applied to the memories to be purged. Filters are defined using disjunctive normal form (OR of ANDs). For example: `filter_groups: [{filters: [{key: "author", value: {string_value: "agent 123"}, op: EQUAL}]}, {filters: [{key: "label", value: {string_value: "travel"}, op: EQUAL}, {key: "author", value: {string_value: "agent 321"}, op: EQUAL}]}]` would be equivalent to the logical expression: `(metadata.author = "agent 123" OR (metadata.label = "travel" AND metadata.author = "agent 321"))`.
